chapters.zh: 哈迦利亚的儿子尼希米的言语如下：亚达薛西王二十年基斯流月，我在书珊城的宫中。
chapters.en: The words of Nehemiah the son of Hachaliah. And it came to pass in the month Chisleu, in the twentieth year, as I was in Shushan the palace,

chapters.zh: 那时，有我一个弟兄哈拿尼，同著几个人从犹大来。我问他们那些被掳归回、剩下逃脱的犹大人和耶路撒冷的光景。
chapters.en: That Hanani, one of my brethren, came, he and men of Judah; and I asked them concerning the Jews that had escaped, which were left of the captivity, and concerning Jerusalem.

chapters.zh: 他们对我说：那些被掳归回剩下的人在犹大省遭大难，受凌辱；并且耶路撒冷的城墙拆毁，城门被火焚烧。
chapters.en: And they said unto me, The remnant that are left of the captivity there in the province in great affliction and reproach: the wall of Jerusalem also broken down, and the gates thereof are burned with fire.

chapters.zh: 我听见这话，就坐下哭泣，悲哀几日，在天上的神面前禁食祈祷，说：
chapters.en: And it came to pass, when I heard these words, that I sat down and wept, and mourned days, and fasted, and prayed before the God of heaven,

chapters.zh: 耶和华 ─ 天上的神，大而可畏的神啊，你向爱你、守你诫命的人守约施慈爱。
chapters.en: And said, I beseech thee, O LORD God of heaven, the great and terrible God, that keepeth covenant and mercy for them that love him and observe his commandments:

chapters.zh: 愿你睁眼看，侧耳听，你仆人昼夜在你面前为你众仆人以色列民的祈祷，承认我们以色列人向你所犯的罪；我与我父家都有罪了。
chapters.en: Let thine ear now be attentive, and thine eyes open, that thou mayest hear the prayer of thy servant, which I pray before thee now, day and night, for the children of Israel thy servants, and confess the sins of the children of Israel, which we have sinned against thee: both I and my father's house have sinned.

chapters.zh: 我们向你所行的甚是邪恶，没有遵守你藉著仆人摩西所吩咐的诫命、律例、典章。
chapters.en: We have dealt very corruptly against thee, and have not kept the commandments, nor the statutes, nor the judgments, which thou commandedst thy servant Moses.

chapters.zh: 求你记念所吩咐你仆人摩西的话，说：你们若犯罪，我就把你们分散在万民中；
chapters.en: Remember, I beseech thee, the word that thou commandedst thy servant Moses, saying, ye transgress, I will scatter you abroad among the nations:

chapters.zh: 但你们若归向我，谨守遵行我的诫命，你们被赶散的人虽在天涯，我也必从那里将他们招聚回来，带到我所选择立为我名的居所。
chapters.en: But ye turn unto me, and keep my commandments, and do them; though there were of you cast out unto the uttermost part of the heaven, will I gather them from thence, and will bring them unto the place that I have chosen to set my name there.

chapters.zh: 这都是你的仆人、你的百姓，就是你用大力和大能的手所救赎的。
chapters.en: Now these thy servants and thy people, whom thou hast redeemed by thy great power, and by thy strong hand.

chapters.zh: 主啊，求你侧耳听你仆人的祈祷，和喜爱敬畏你名众仆人的祈祷，使你仆人现今亨通，在王面前蒙恩。我是作王酒政的。
chapters.en: O Lord, I beseech thee, let now thine ear be attentive to the prayer of thy servant, and to the prayer of thy servants, who desire to fear thy name: and prosper, I pray thee, thy servant this day, and grant him mercy in the sight of this man. For I was the king's cupbearer.

chapters.zh: 亚达薛西王二十年尼散月，在王面前摆酒，我拿起酒来奉给王。我素来在王面前没有愁容。
chapters.en: And it came to pass in the month Nisan, in the twentieth year of Artaxerxes the king, wine before him: and I took up the wine, and gave unto the king. Now I had not been sad in his presence.

chapters.zh: 王对我说：你既没有病，为甚么面带愁容呢？这不是别的，必是你心中愁烦。于是我甚惧怕。
chapters.en: Wherefore the king said unto me, Why thy countenance sad, seeing thou not sick? this nothing but sorrow of heart. Then I was very sore afraid,

chapters.zh: 我对王说：愿王万岁！我列祖坟墓所在的那城荒凉，城门被火焚烧，我岂能面无愁容么？
chapters.en: And said unto the king, Let the king live for ever: why should not my countenance be sad, when the city, the place of my fathers' sepulchres, waste, and the gates thereof are consumed with fire?

chapters.zh: 王问我说：你要求甚么？于是我默祷天上的神。
chapters.en: Then the king said unto me, For what dost thou make request? So I prayed to the God of heaven.

chapters.zh: 我对王说：仆人若在王眼前蒙恩，王若喜欢，求王差遣我往犹大，到我列祖坟墓所在的那城去，我好重新建造。
chapters.en: And I said unto the king, If it please the king, and if thy servant have found favour in thy sight, that thou wouldest send me unto Judah, unto the city of my fathers' sepulchres, that I may build it.

chapters.zh: 那时王后坐在王的旁边。王问我说：你去要多少日子？几时回来？我就定了日期。于是王喜欢差遣我去。
chapters.en: And the king said unto me, (the queen also sitting by him,) For how long shall thy journey be? and when wilt thou return? So it pleased the king to send me; and I set him a time.

chapters.zh: 我又对王说：王若喜欢，求王赐我诏书，通知大河西的省长准我经过，直到犹大；
chapters.en: Moreover I said unto the king, If it please the king, let letters be given me to the governors beyond the river, that they may convey me over till I come into Judah;

chapters.zh: 又赐诏书，通知管理王园林的亚萨，使他给我木料，做属殿营楼之门的横梁和城墙，与我自己房屋使用的。王就允准我，因我神施恩的手帮助我。
chapters.en: And a letter unto Asaph the keeper of the king's forest, that he may give me timber to make beams for the gates of the palace which to the house, and for the wall of the city, and for the house that I shall enter into. And the king granted me, according to the good hand of my God upon me.

chapters.zh: 王派了军长和马兵护送我。我到了河西的省长那里，将王的诏书交给他们。
chapters.en: Then I came to the governors beyond the river, and gave them the king's letters. Now the king had sent captains of the army and horsemen with me.

chapters.zh: 和伦人参巴拉，并为奴的亚扪人多比雅，听见有人来为以色列人求好处，就甚恼怒。
chapters.en: When Sanballat the Horonite, and Tobiah the servant, the Ammonite, heard , it grieved them exceedingly that there was come a man to seek the welfare of the children of Israel.

chapters.zh: 我到了耶路撒冷，在那里住了三日。
chapters.en: So I came to Jerusalem, and was there three days.

chapters.zh: 我夜间起来，有几个人也一同起来；但神使我心里要为耶路撒冷做甚么事，我并没有告诉人。除了我骑的牲口以外，也没有别的牲口在我那里。
chapters.en: And I arose in the night, I and some few men with me; neither told I man what my God had put in my heart to do at Jerusalem: neither beast with me, save the beast that I rode upon.

chapters.zh: 当夜我出了谷门，往野狗井去（野狗：或译龙），到了粪厂门，察看耶路撒冷的城墙，见城墙拆毁，城门被火焚烧。
chapters.en: And I went out by night by the gate of the valley, even before the dragon well, and to the dung port, and viewed the walls of Jerusalem, which were broken down, and the gates thereof were consumed with fire.

chapters.zh: 我又往前，到了泉门和王池，但所骑的牲口没有地方过去。
chapters.en: Then I went on to the gate of the fountain, and to the king's pool: but no place for the beast under me to pass.

chapters.zh: 于是夜间沿溪而上，察看城墙，又转身进入谷门，就回来了。
chapters.en: Then went I up in the night by the brook, and viewed the wall, and turned back, and entered by the gate of the valley, and returned.

chapters.zh: 我往哪里去，我做甚么事，官长都不知道。我还没有告诉犹大平民、祭司、贵胄、官长，和其余做工的人。
chapters.en: And the rulers knew not whither I went, or what I did; neither had I as yet told to the Jews, nor to the priests, nor to the nobles, nor to the rulers, nor to the rest that did the work.

chapters.zh: 以后，我对他们说：我们所遭的难，耶路撒冷怎样荒凉，城门被火焚烧，你们都看见了。来罢，我们重建耶路撒冷的城墙，免得再受凌辱！
chapters.en: Then said I unto them, Ye see the distress that we in, how Jerusalem waste, and the gates thereof are burned with fire: come, and let us build up the wall of Jerusalem, that we be no more a reproach.

chapters.zh: 我告诉他们我神施恩的手怎样帮助我，并王对我所说的话。他们就说：我们起来建造罢！于是他们奋勇做这善工。
chapters.en: Then I told them of the hand of my God which was good upon me; as also the king's words that he had spoken unto me. And they said, Let us rise up and build. So they strengthened their hands for good .

chapters.zh: 但和伦人参巴拉，并为奴的亚扪人多比雅和亚拉伯人基善听见就嗤笑我们，藐视我们，说：你们做甚么呢？要背叛王么？
chapters.en: But when Sanballat the Horonite, and Tobiah the servant, the Ammonite, and Geshem the Arabian, heard , they laughed us to scorn, and despised us, and said, What this thing that ye do? will ye rebel against the king?

chapters.zh: 我回答他们说：天上的神必使我们亨通。我们作他仆人的，要起来建造；你们却在耶路撒冷无分、无权、无纪念。
chapters.en: Then answered I them, and said unto them, The God of heaven, he will prosper us; therefore we his servants will arise and build: but ye have no portion, nor right, nor memorial, in Jerusalem.

chapters.zh: 那时，大祭司以利亚实和他的弟兄众祭司起来建立羊门，分别为圣，安立门扇，又筑城墙到哈米亚楼，直到哈楠业楼，分别为圣。
chapters.en: Then Eliashib the high priest rose up with his brethren the priests, and they builded the sheep gate; they sanctified it, and set up the doors of it; even unto the tower of Meah they sanctified it, unto the tower of Hananeel.

chapters.zh: 其次是耶利哥人建造。其次是音利的儿子撒刻建造。
chapters.en: And next unto him builded the men of Jericho. And next to them builded Zaccur the son of Imri.

chapters.zh: 哈西拿的子孙建立鱼门，架横梁、安门扇，和闩锁。
chapters.en: But the fish gate did the sons of Hassenaah build, who laid the beams thereof, and set up the doors thereof, the locks thereof, and the bars thereof.

chapters.zh: 其次是哈哥斯的孙子、乌利亚的儿子米利末修造。其次是米示萨别的孙子、比利迦的儿子米书兰修造。其次是巴拿的儿子撒督修造。
chapters.en: And next unto them repaired Meremoth the son of Urijah, the son of Koz. And next unto them repaired Meshullam the son of Berechiah, the son of Meshezabeel. And next unto them repaired Zadok the son of Baana.

chapters.zh: 其次是提哥亚人修造；但是他们的贵胄不用肩（原文是颈项）担他们主的工作。
chapters.en: And next unto them the Tekoites repaired; but their nobles put not their necks to the work of their Lord.

chapters.zh: 巴西亚的儿子耶何耶大与比所玳的儿子米书兰修造古门，架横梁，安门扇和闩锁。
chapters.en: Moreover the old gate repaired Jehoiada the son of Paseah, and Meshullam the son of Besodeiah; they laid the beams thereof, and set up the doors thereof, and the locks thereof, and the bars thereof.

chapters.zh: 其次是基遍人米拉提，米伦人雅顿与基遍人，并属河西总督所管的米斯巴人修造。
chapters.en: And next unto them repaired Melatiah the Gibeonite, and Jadon the Meronothite, the men of Gibeon, and of Mizpah, unto the throne of the governor on this side the river.

chapters.zh: 其次是银匠哈海雅的儿子乌薛修造。其次是做香的哈拿尼雅修造。这些人修坚耶路撒冷，直到宽墙。
chapters.en: Next unto him repaired Uzziel the son of Harhaiah, of the goldsmiths. Next unto him also repaired Hananiah the son of the apothecaries, and they fortified Jerusalem unto the broad wall.

chapters.zh: 其次是管理耶路撒冷一半、户珥的儿子利法雅修造。
chapters.en: And next unto them repaired Rephaiah the son of Hur, the ruler of the half part of Jerusalem.

chapters.zh: 其次是哈路抹的儿子耶大雅对著自己的房屋修造。其次是哈沙尼的儿子哈突修造。
chapters.en: And next unto them repaired Jedaiah the son of Harumaph, even over against his house. And next unto him repaired Hattush the son of Hashabniah.

chapters.zh: 哈琳的儿子玛基雅和巴哈摩押的儿子哈述修造一段，并修造炉楼。
chapters.en: Malchijah the son of Harim, and Hashub the son of Pahathmoab, repaired the other piece, and the tower of the furnaces.

chapters.zh: 其次是管理耶路撒冷那一半、哈罗黑的儿子沙龙和他的女儿们修造。
chapters.en: And next unto him repaired Shallum the son of Halohesh, the ruler of the half part of Jerusalem, he and his daughters.

chapters.zh: 哈嫩和撒挪亚的居民修造谷门，立门，安门扇和闩锁，又建筑城墙一千肘，直到粪厂门。
chapters.en: The valley gate repaired Hanun, and the inhabitants of Zanoah; they built it, and set up the doors thereof, the locks thereof, and the bars thereof, and a thousand cubits on the wall unto the dung gate.

chapters.zh: 管理伯哈基琳、利甲的儿子玛基雅修造粪厂门，立门，安门扇和闩锁。
chapters.en: But the dung gate repaired Malchiah the son of Rechab, the ruler of part of Bethhaccerem; he built it, and set up the doors thereof, the locks thereof, and the bars thereof.

chapters.zh: 管理米斯巴、各荷西的儿子沙仑修造泉门，立门，盖门顶，安门扇和闩锁，又修造靠近王园西罗亚池的墙垣，直到那从大卫城下来的台阶。
chapters.en: But the gate of the fountain repaired Shallun the son of Colhozeh, the ruler of part of Mizpah; he built it, and covered it, and set up the doors thereof, the locks thereof, and the bars thereof, and the wall of the pool of Siloah by the king's garden, and unto the stairs that go down from the city of David.

chapters.zh: 其次是管理伯夙一半、押卜的儿子尼希米修造，直到大卫坟地的对面，又到挖成的池子，并勇士的房屋。
chapters.en: After him repaired Nehemiah the son of Azbuk, the ruler of the half part of Bethzur, unto over against the sepulchres of David, and to the pool that was made, and unto the house of the mighty.

chapters.zh: 其次是利未人巴尼的儿子利宏修造。其次是管理基伊拉一半、哈沙比雅为他所管的本境修造。
chapters.en: After him repaired the Levites, Rehum the son of Bani. Next unto him repaired Hashabiah, the ruler of the half part of Keilah, in his part.

chapters.zh: 其次是利未人弟兄中管理基伊拉那一半、希拿达的儿子巴瓦伊修造。
chapters.en: After him repaired their brethren, Bavai the son of Henadad, the ruler of the half part of Keilah.

chapters.zh: 其次是管理米斯巴、耶书亚的儿子以谢修造一段，对著武库的上坡、城墙转弯之处。
chapters.en: And next to him repaired Ezer the son of Jeshua, the ruler of Mizpah, another piece over against the going up to the armoury at the turning .

chapters.zh: 其次是萨拜的儿子巴录竭力修造一段，从城墙转弯，直到大祭司以利亚实的府门。
chapters.en: After him Baruch the son of Zabbai earnestly repaired the other piece, from the turning unto the door of the house of Eliashib the high priest.

chapters.zh: 其次是哈哥斯的孙子、乌利亚的儿子米利末修造一段，从以利亚实的府门，直到以利亚实府的尽头。
chapters.en: After him repaired Meremoth the son of Urijah the son of Koz another piece, from the door of the house of Eliashib even to the end of the house of Eliashib.

chapters.zh: 其次是住平原的祭司修造。
chapters.en: And after him repaired the priests, the men of the plain.

chapters.zh: 其次是便雅悯与哈述对著自己的房屋修造。其次是亚难尼的孙子、玛西雅的儿子亚撒利雅在靠近自己的房屋修造。
chapters.en: After him repaired Benjamin and Hashub over against their house. After him repaired Azariah the son of Maaseiah the son of Ananiah by his house.

chapters.zh: 其次是希拿达的儿子宾内修造一段，从亚撒利雅的房屋直到城墙转弯，又到城角。
chapters.en: After him repaired Binnui the son of Henadad another piece, from the house of Azariah unto the turning , even unto the corner.

chapters.zh: 乌赛的儿子巴拉修造对著城墙的转弯和王上宫凸出来的城楼，靠近护卫院的那一段。其次是巴录的儿子毗大雅修造。
chapters.en: Palal the son of Uzai, over against the turning , and the tower which lieth out from the king's high house, that by the court of the prison. After him Pedaiah the son of Parosh.

chapters.zh: （尼提宁住在俄斐勒，直到朝东水门的对面和凸出来的城楼。）
chapters.en: Moreover the Nethinims dwelt in Ophel, unto over against the water gate toward the east, and the tower that lieth out.

chapters.zh: 其次是提哥亚人又修一段，对著那凸出来的大楼，直到俄斐勒的墙。
chapters.en: After them the Tekoites repaired another piece, over against the great tower that lieth out, even unto the wall of Ophel.

chapters.zh: 从马门往上，众祭司各对自己的房屋修造。
chapters.en: From above the horse gate repaired the priests, every one over against his house.

chapters.zh: 其次是音麦的儿子撒督对著自己的房屋修造。其次是守东门、示迦尼的儿子示玛雅修造。
chapters.en: After them repaired Zadok the son of Immer over against his house. After him repaired also Shemaiah the son of Shechaniah, the keeper of the east gate.

chapters.zh: 其次是示利米雅的儿子哈拿尼雅和萨拉的第六子哈嫩又修一段。其次是比利迦的儿子米书兰对著自己的房屋修造。
chapters.en: After him repaired Hananiah the son of Shelemiah, and Hanun the sixth son of Zalaph, another piece. After him repaired Meshullam the son of Berechiah over against his chamber.

chapters.zh: 其次是银匠玛基雅修造到尼提宁和商人的房屋，对著哈米弗甲门，直到城的角楼。
chapters.en: After him repaired Malchiah the goldsmith's son unto the place of the Nethinims, and of the merchants, over against the gate Miphkad, and to the going up of the corner.

chapters.zh: 银匠与商人在城的角楼和羊门中间修造。
chapters.en: And between the going up of the corner unto the sheep gate repaired the goldsmiths and the merchants.

chapters.zh: 参巴拉听见我们修造城墙就发怒，大大恼恨，嗤笑犹大人，
chapters.en: But it came to pass, that when Sanballat heard that we builded the wall, he was wroth, and took great indignation, and mocked the Jews.

chapters.zh: 对他弟兄和撒玛利亚的军兵说：这些软弱的犹大人做甚么呢？要保护自己么？要献祭么？要一日成功么？要从土堆里拿出火烧的石头再立墙么？
chapters.en: And he spake before his brethren and the army of Samaria, and said, What do these feeble Jews? will they fortify themselves? will they sacrifice? will they make an end in a day? will they revive the stones out of the heaps of the rubbish which are burned?

chapters.zh: 亚扪人多比雅站在旁边，说：他们所修造的石墙，就是狐狸上去也必跐倒。
chapters.en: Now Tobiah the Ammonite by him, and he said, Even that which they build, if a fox go up, he shall even break down their stone wall.

chapters.zh: 我们的神啊，求你垂听，因为我们被藐视。求你使他们的毁谤归于他们的头上，使他们在掳到之地作为掠物。
chapters.en: Hear, O our God; for we are despised: and turn their reproach upon their own head, and give them for a prey in the land of captivity:

chapters.zh: 不要遮掩他们的罪孽，不要使他们的罪恶从你面前涂抹，因为他们在修造的人眼前惹动你的怒气。
chapters.en: And cover not their iniquity, and let not their sin be blotted out from before thee: for they have provoked to anger before the builders.

chapters.zh: 这样，我们修造城墙，城墙就都连络，高至一半，因为百姓专心做工。
chapters.en: So built we the wall; and all the wall was joined together unto the half thereof: for the people had a mind to work.

chapters.zh: 参巴拉、多比雅、亚拉伯人、亚扪人、亚实突人听见修造耶路撒冷城墙，著手进行堵塞破裂的地方，就甚发怒。
chapters.en: But it came to pass, when Sanballat, and Tobiah, and the Arabians, and the Ammonites, and the Ashdodites, heard that the walls of Jerusalem were made up, that the breaches began to be stopped, then they were very wroth,

chapters.zh: 大家同谋要来攻击耶路撒冷，使城内扰乱。
chapters.en: And conspired all of them together to come to fight against Jerusalem, and to hinder it.

chapters.zh: 然而，我们祷告我们的神，又因他们的缘故，就派人看守，昼夜防备。
chapters.en: Nevertheless we made our prayer unto our God, and set a watch against them day and night, because of them.

chapters.zh: 犹大人说：灰土尚多，扛抬的人力气已经衰败，所以我们不能建造城墙。
chapters.en: And Judah said, The strength of the bearers of burdens is decayed, and much rubbish; so that we are not able to build the wall.

chapters.zh: 我们的敌人且说：趁他们不知不见，我们进入他们中间，杀他们，使工作止住。
chapters.en: And our adversaries said, They shall not know, neither see, till we come in the midst among them, and slay them, and cause the work to cease.

chapters.zh: 那靠近敌人居住的犹大人十次从各处来见我们，说：你们必要回到我们那里。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, that when the Jews which dwelt by them came, they said unto us ten times, From all places whence ye shall return unto us .

chapters.zh: 所以我使百姓各按宗族拿刀、拿枪、拿弓站在城墙后边低洼的空处。
chapters.en: Therefore set I in the lower places behind the wall, on the higher places, I even set the people after their families with their swords, their spears, and their bows.

chapters.zh: 我察看了，就起来对贵胄、官长，和其余的人说：不要怕他们！当记念主是大而可畏的。你们要为弟兄、儿女、妻子、家产争战。
chapters.en: And I looked, and rose up, and said unto the nobles, and to the rulers, and to the rest of the people, Be not ye afraid of them: remember the Lord, great and terrible, and fight for your brethren, your sons, and your daughters, your wives, and your houses.

chapters.zh: 仇敌听见我们知道他们的心意，见神也破坏他们的计谋，就不来了。我们都回到城墙那里，各做各的工。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, when our enemies heard that it was known unto us, and God had brought their counsel to nought, that we returned all of us to the wall, every one unto his work.

chapters.zh: 从那日起，我的仆人一半做工，一半拿枪、拿盾牌、拿弓、穿（或译：拿）铠甲，官长都站在犹大众人的后边。
chapters.en: And it came to pass from that time forth, the half of my servants wrought in the work, and the other half of them held both the spears, the shields, and the bows, and the habergeons; and the rulers behind all the house of Judah.

chapters.zh: 修造城墙的，扛抬材料的，都一手做工一手拿兵器。
chapters.en: They which builded on the wall, and they that bare burdens, with those that laded, with one of his hands wrought in the work, and with the other held a weapon.

chapters.zh: 修造的人都腰间佩刀修造，吹角的人在我旁边。
chapters.en: For the builders, every one had his sword girded by his side, and builded. And he that sounded the trumpet by me.

chapters.zh: 我对贵胄、官长，和其余的人说：这工程浩大，我们在城墙上相离甚远；
chapters.en: And I said unto the nobles, and to the rulers, and to the rest of the people, The work great and large, and we are separated upon the wall, one far from another.

chapters.zh: 你们听见角声在哪里，就聚集到我们那里去。我们的神必为我们争战。
chapters.en: In what place ye hear the sound of the trumpet, resort ye thither unto us: our God shall fight for us.

chapters.zh: 于是，我们做工，一半拿兵器，从天亮直到星宿出现的时候。
chapters.en: So we laboured in the work: and half of them held the spears from the rising of the morning till the stars appeared.

chapters.zh: 那时，我又对百姓说：各人和他的仆人当在耶路撒冷住宿，好在夜间保守我们，白昼做工。
chapters.en: Likewise at the same time said I unto the people, Let every one with his servant lodge within Jerusalem, that in the night they may be a guard to us, and labour on the day.

chapters.zh: 这样，我和弟兄仆人，并跟从我的护兵都不脱衣服，出去打水也带兵器。
chapters.en: So neither I, nor my brethren, nor my servants, nor the men of the guard which followed me, none of us put off our clothes, every one put them off for washing.

chapters.zh: 百姓和他们的妻大大呼号，埋怨他们的弟兄犹大人。
chapters.en: And there was a great cry of the people and of their wives against their brethren the Jews.

chapters.zh: 有的说：我们和儿女人口众多，要去得粮食度命；
chapters.en: For there were that said, We, our sons, and our daughters, many: therefore we take up corn , that we may eat, and live.

chapters.zh: 有的说：我们典了田地、葡萄园、房屋，要得粮食充饥；
chapters.en: also there were that said, We have mortgaged our lands, vineyards, and houses, that we might buy corn, because of the dearth.

chapters.zh: 有的说：我们已经指著田地、葡萄园，借了钱给王纳税。
chapters.en: There were also that said, We have borrowed money for the king's tribute, our lands and vineyards.

chapters.zh: 我们的身体与我们弟兄的身体一样；我们的儿女与他们的儿女一般。现在我们将要使儿女作人的仆婢，我们的女儿已有为婢的；我们并无力拯救，因为我们的田地、葡萄园已经归了别人。
chapters.en: Yet now our flesh as the flesh of our brethren, our children as their children: and, lo, we bring into bondage our sons and our daughters to be servants, and of our daughters are brought unto bondage : neither in our power ; for other men have our lands and vineyards.

chapters.zh: 我听见他们呼号说这些话，便甚发怒。
chapters.en: And I was very angry when I heard their cry and these words.

chapters.zh: 我心里筹划，就斥责贵胄和官长说：你们各人向弟兄取利！于是我招聚大会攻击他们。
chapters.en: Then I consulted with myself, and I rebuked the nobles, and the rulers, and said unto them, Ye exact usury, every one of his brother. And I set a great assembly against them.

chapters.zh: 我对他们说：我们尽力赎回我们弟兄，就是卖与外邦的犹大人；你们还要卖弟兄，使我们赎回来么？他们就静默不语，无话可答。
chapters.en: And I said unto them, We after our ability have redeemed our brethren the Jews, which were sold unto the heathen; and will ye even sell your brethren? or shall they be sold unto us? Then held they their peace, and found nothing .

chapters.zh: 我又说：你们所行的不善！你们行事不当敬畏我们的神么？不然，难免我们的仇敌外邦人毁谤我们。
chapters.en: Also I said, It not good that ye do: ought ye not to walk in the fear of our God because of the reproach of the heathen our enemies?

chapters.zh: 我和我的弟兄与仆人也将银钱粮食借给百姓；我们大家都当免去利息。
chapters.en: I likewise, my brethren, and my servants, might exact of them money and corn: I pray you, let us leave off this usury.

chapters.zh: 如今我劝你们将他们的田地、葡萄园、橄榄园、房屋，并向他们所取的银钱、粮食、新酒，和油，百分之一的利息都归还他们。
chapters.en: Restore, I pray you, to them, even this day, their lands, their vineyards, their oliveyards, and their houses, also the hundredth of the money, and of the corn, the wine, and the oil, that ye exact of them.

chapters.zh: 众人说：我们必归还，不再向他们索要，必照你的话行。我就召了祭司来，叫众人起誓，必照著所应许的而行。
chapters.en: Then said they, We will restore , and will require nothing of them; so will we do as thou sayest. Then I called the priests, and took an oath of them, that they should do according to this promise.

chapters.zh: 我也抖著胸前的衣襟，说：凡不成就这应许的，愿神照样抖他离开家产和他劳碌得来的，直到抖空了。会众都说：阿们！又赞美耶和华。百姓就照著所应许的去行。
chapters.en: Also I shook my lap, and said, So God shake out every man from his house, and from his labour, that performeth not this promise, even thus be he shaken out, and emptied. And all the congregation said, Amen, and praised the LORD. And the people did according to this promise.

chapters.zh: 自从我奉派作犹大地的省长，就是从亚达薛西王二十年直到三十二年，共十二年之久，我与我弟兄都没有吃省长的俸禄。
chapters.en: Moreover from the time that I was appointed to be their governor in the land of Judah, from the twentieth year even unto the two and thirtieth year of Artaxerxes the king, , twelve years, I and my brethren have not eaten the bread of the governor.

chapters.zh: 在我以前的省长加重百姓的担子，每日索要粮食和酒，并银子四十舍客勒，就是他们的仆人也辖制百姓；但我因敬畏神不这样行。
chapters.en: But the former governors that before me were chargeable unto the people, and had taken of them bread and wine, beside forty shekels of silver; yea, even their servants bare rule over the people: but so did not I, because of the fear of God.

chapters.zh: 并且我恒心修造城墙，并没有置买田地；我的仆人也都聚集在那里做工。
chapters.en: Yea, also I continued in the work of this wall, neither bought we any land: and all my servants gathered thither unto the work.

chapters.zh: 除了从四围外邦中来的犹大人以外，有犹大平民和官长一百五十人在我席上吃饭。
chapters.en: Moreover at my table an hundred and fifty of the Jews and rulers, beside those that came unto us from among the heathen that about us.

chapters.zh: 每日预备一只公牛，六只肥羊，又预备些飞禽；每十日一次，多预备各样的酒。虽然如此，我并不要省长的俸禄，因为百姓服役甚重。
chapters.en: Now which was prepared daily one ox six choice sheep; also fowls were prepared for me, and once in ten days store of all sorts of wine: yet for all this required not I the bread of the governor, because the bondage was heavy upon this people.

chapters.zh: 我的神啊，求你记念我为这百姓所行的一切事，施恩与我。
chapters.en: Think upon me, my God, for good, to all that I have done for this people.

chapters.zh: 参巴拉、多比雅、亚拉伯人基善和我们其余的仇敌听见我已经修完了城墙，其中没有破裂之处（那时我还没有安门扇），
chapters.en: Now it came to pass, when Sanballat, and Tobiah, and Geshem the Arabian, and the rest of our enemies, heard that I had builded the wall, and there was no breach left therein; (though at that time I had not set up the doors upon the gates;)

chapters.zh: 参巴拉和基善就打发人来见我，说：请你来，我们在阿挪平原的一个村庄相会。他们却想害我。
chapters.en: That Sanballat and Geshem sent unto me, saying, Come, let us meet together in the villages in the plain of Ono. But they thought to do me mischief.

chapters.zh: 于是我差遣人去见他们，说：我现在办理大工，不能下去。焉能停工下去见你们呢？
chapters.en: And I sent messengers unto them, saying, I doing a great work, so that I cannot come down: why should the work cease, whilst I leave it, and come down to you?

chapters.zh: 他们这样四次打发人来见我，我都如此回答他们。
chapters.en: Yet they sent unto me four times after this sort; and I answered them after the same manner.

chapters.zh: 参巴拉第五次打发仆人来见我，手里拿著未封的信，
chapters.en: Then sent Sanballat his servant unto me in like manner the fifth time with an open letter in his hand;

chapters.zh: 信上写著说：外邦人中有风声，迦施慕（就是基善，见二章十九节）也说，你和犹大人谋反，修造城墙，你要作他们的王；
chapters.en: Wherein written, It is reported among the heathen, and Gashmu saith thou and the Jews think to rebel: for which cause thou buildest the wall, that thou mayest be their king, according to these words.

chapters.zh: 你又派先知在耶路撒冷指著你宣讲，说在犹大有王。现在这话必传与王知；所以请你来，与我们彼此商议。
chapters.en: And thou hast also appointed prophets to preach of thee at Jerusalem, saying, a king in Judah: and now shall it be reported to the king according to these words. Come now therefore, and let us take counsel together.

chapters.zh: 我就差遣人去见他，说：你所说的这事，一概没有，是你心里捏造的。
chapters.en: Then I sent unto him, saying, There are no such things done as thou sayest, but thou feignest them out of thine own heart.

chapters.zh: 他们都要使我们惧怕，意思说，他们的手必软弱，以致工作不能成就。神啊，求你坚固我的手。
chapters.en: For they all made us afraid, saying, Their hands shall be weakened from the work, that it be not done. Now therefore, , strengthen my hands.

chapters.zh: 我到了米希大别的孙子、第来雅的儿子示玛雅家里；那时，他闭门不出。他说：我们不如在神的殿里会面，将殿门关锁；因为他们要来杀你，就是夜里来杀你。
chapters.en: Afterward I came unto the house of Shemaiah the son of Delaiah the son of Mehetabeel, who shut up; and he said, Let us meet together in the house of God, within the temple, and let us shut the doors of the temple: for they will come to slay thee; yea, in the night will they come to slay thee.

chapters.zh: 我说：像我这样的人岂要逃跑呢？像我这样的人岂能进入殿里保全生命呢？我不进去！
chapters.en: And I said, Should such a man as I flee? and who , that, as I , would go into the temple to save his life? I will not go in.

chapters.zh: 我看明神没有差遣他，是他自己说这话攻击我，是多比雅和参巴拉贿买了他。
chapters.en: And, lo, I perceived that God had not sent him; but that he pronounced this prophecy against me: for Tobiah and Sanballat had hired him.

chapters.zh: 贿买他的缘故，是要叫我惧怕，依从他犯罪，他们好传扬恶言毁谤我。
chapters.en: Therefore he hired, that I should be afraid, and do so, and sin, and they might have for an evil report, that they might reproach me.

chapters.zh: 我的神啊，多比雅、参巴拉、女先知挪亚底，和其余的先知要叫我惧怕，求你记念他们所行的这些事。
chapters.en: My God, think thou upon Tobiah and Sanballat according to these their works, and on the prophetess Noadiah, and the rest of the prophets, that would have put me in fear.

chapters.zh: 以禄月二十五日，城墙修完了，共修了五十二天。
chapters.en: So the wall was finished in the twenty and fifth of Elul, in fifty and two days.

chapters.zh: 我们一切仇敌、四围的外邦人听见了便惧怕，愁眉不展；因为见这工作完成是出乎我们的神。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, that when all our enemies heard , and all the heathen that about us saw , they were much cast down in their own eyes: for they perceived that this work was wrought of our God.

chapters.zh: 在那些日子，犹大的贵胄屡次寄信与多比雅，多比雅也来信与他们。
chapters.en: Moreover in those days the nobles of Judah sent many letters unto Tobiah, and of Tobiah came unto them.

chapters.zh: 在犹大有许多人与多比雅结盟；因他是亚拉的儿子，示迦尼的女婿，并且他的儿子约哈难娶了比利迦儿子米书兰的女儿为妻。
chapters.en: For many in Judah sworn unto him, because he the son in law of Shechaniah the son of Arah; and his son Johanan had taken the daughter of Meshullam the son of Berechiah.

chapters.zh: 他们常在我面前说多比雅的善行，也将我的话传与他。多比雅又常寄信来，要叫我惧怕。
chapters.en: Also they reported his good deeds before me, and uttered my words to him. Tobiah sent letters to put me in fear.

chapters.zh: 城墙修完，我安了门扇，守门的、歌唱的，和利未人都已派定。
chapters.en: Now it came to pass, when the wall was built, and I had set up the doors, and the porters and the singers and the Levites were appointed,

chapters.zh: 我就派我的弟兄哈拿尼和营楼的宰官哈拿尼雅管理耶路撒冷；因为哈拿尼雅是忠信的，又敬畏神过于众人。
chapters.en: That I gave my brother Hanani, and Hananiah the ruler of the palace, charge over Jerusalem: for he a faithful man, and feared God above many.

chapters.zh: 我吩咐他们说：等到太阳上升才可开耶路撒冷的城门；人尚看守的时候就要关门上闩；也当派耶路撒冷的居民各按班次看守自己房屋对面之处。
chapters.en: And I said unto them, Let not the gates of Jerusalem be opened until the sun be hot; and while they stand by, let them shut the doors, and bar : and appoint watches of the inhabitants of Jerusalem, every one in his watch, and every one over against his house.

chapters.zh: 城是广大，其中的民却稀少，房屋还没有建造。
chapters.en: Now the city large and great: but the people few therein, and the houses not builded.

chapters.zh: 我的神感动我心，招聚贵胄、官长，和百姓，要照家谱计算。我找著第一次上来之人的家谱，其上写著：
chapters.en: And my God put into mine heart to gather together the nobles, and the rulers, and the people, that they might be reckoned by genealogy. And I found a register of the genealogy of them which came up at the first, and found written therein,

chapters.zh: 巴比伦王尼布甲尼撒从前掳去犹大省的人，现在他们的子孙从被掳到之地回耶路撒冷和犹大，各归本城。
chapters.en: These the children of the province, that went up out of the captivity, of those that had been carried away, whom Nebuchadnezzar the king of Babylon had carried away, and came again to Jerusalem and to Judah, every one unto his city;

chapters.zh: 他们是同著所罗巴伯、耶书亚、尼希米、亚撒利雅、拉米、拿哈玛尼、末底改、必珊、米斯毗列、比革瓦伊、尼宏、巴拿回来的。
chapters.en: Who came with Zerubbabel, Jeshua, Nehemiah, Azariah, Raamiah, Nahamani, Mordecai, Bilshan, Mispereth, Bigvai, Nehum, Baanah. The number, , of the men of the people of Israel ;

chapters.zh: 以色列人民的数目记在下面：巴录的子孙二千一百七十二名；
chapters.en: The children of Parosh, two thousand an hundred seventy and two.

chapters.zh: 示法提雅的子孙三百七十二名；
chapters.en: The children of Shephatiah, three hundred seventy and two.

chapters.zh: 亚拉的子孙六百五十二名；
chapters.en: The children of Arah, six hundred fifty and two.

chapters.zh: 巴哈摩押的后裔，就是耶书亚和约押的子孙二千八百一十八名；
chapters.en: The children of Pahathmoab, of the children of Jeshua and Joab, two thousand and eight hundred eighteen.

chapters.zh: 以拦的子孙一千二百五十四名；
chapters.en: The children of Elam, a thousand two hundred fifty and four.

chapters.zh: 萨土的子孙八百四十五名；
chapters.en: The children of Zattu, eight hundred forty and five.

chapters.zh: 萨改的子孙七百六十名；
chapters.en: The children of Zaccai, seven hundred and threescore.

chapters.zh: 宾内的子孙六百四十八名；
chapters.en: The children of Binnui, six hundred forty and eight.

chapters.zh: 比拜的子孙六百二十八名；
chapters.en: The children of Bebai, six hundred twenty and eight.

chapters.zh: 押甲的子孙二千三百二十二名；
chapters.en: The children of Azgad, two thousand three hundred twenty and two.

chapters.zh: 亚多尼干的子孙六百六十七名；
chapters.en: The children of Adonikam, six hundred threescore and seven.

chapters.zh: 比革瓦伊的子孙二千零六十七名；
chapters.en: The children of Bigvai, two thousand threescore and seven.

chapters.zh: 亚丁的子孙六百五十五名；
chapters.en: The children of Adin, six hundred fifty and five.

chapters.zh: 亚特的后裔，就是希西家的子孙九十八名；
chapters.en: The children of Ater of Hezekiah, ninety and eight.

chapters.zh: 哈顺的子孙三百二十八名；
chapters.en: The children of Hashum, three hundred twenty and eight.

chapters.zh: 比赛的子孙三百二十四名；
chapters.en: The children of Bezai, three hundred twenty and four.

chapters.zh: 哈拉的子孙一百一十二名；
chapters.en: The children of Hariph, an hundred and twelve.

chapters.zh: 基遍人九十五名；
chapters.en: The children of Gibeon, ninety and five.

chapters.zh: 伯利恒人和尼陀法人共一百八十八名；
chapters.en: The men of Bethlehem and Netophah, an hundred fourscore and eight.

chapters.zh: 亚拿突人一百二十八名；
chapters.en: The men of Anathoth, an hundred twenty and eight.

chapters.zh: 伯亚斯玛弗人四十二名；
chapters.en: The men of Bethazmaveth, forty and two.

chapters.zh: 基列耶琳人、基非拉人、比录人共七百四十三名；
chapters.en: The men of Kirjathjearim, Chephirah, and Beeroth, seven hundred forty and three.

chapters.zh: 拉玛人和迦巴人共六百二十一名；
chapters.en: The men of Ramah and Geba, six hundred twenty and one.

chapters.zh: 默玛人一百二十二名；
chapters.en: The men of Michmas, an hundred and twenty and two.

chapters.zh: 伯特利人和艾人共一百二十三名；
chapters.en: The men of Bethel and Ai, an hundred twenty and three.

chapters.zh: 别的尼波人五十二名；
chapters.en: The men of the other Nebo, fifty and two.

chapters.zh: 别的以拦子孙一千二百五十四名；
chapters.en: The children of the other Elam, a thousand two hundred fifty and four.

chapters.zh: 哈琳的子孙三百二十名；
chapters.en: The children of Harim, three hundred and twenty.

chapters.zh: 耶利哥人三百四十五名；
chapters.en: The children of Jericho, three hundred forty and five.

chapters.zh: 罗德人、哈第人、阿挪人共七百二十一名；
chapters.en: The children of Lod, Hadid, and Ono, seven hundred twenty and one.

chapters.zh: 西拿人三千九百三十名。
chapters.en: The children of Senaah, three thousand nine hundred and thirty.

chapters.zh: 祭司：耶书亚家，耶大雅的子孙九百七十三名；
chapters.en: The priests: the children of Jedaiah, of the house of Jeshua, nine hundred seventy and three.

chapters.zh: 音麦的子孙一千零五十二名；
chapters.en: The children of Immer, a thousand fifty and two.

chapters.zh: 巴施户珥的子孙一千二百四十七名；
chapters.en: The children of Pashur, a thousand two hundred forty and seven.

chapters.zh: 哈琳的子孙一千零一十七名。
chapters.en: The children of Harim, a thousand and seventeen.

chapters.zh: 利未人：何达威的后裔，就是耶书亚和甲篾的子孙七十四名。
chapters.en: The Levites: the children of Jeshua, of Kadmiel, of the children of Hodevah, seventy and four.

chapters.zh: 歌唱的：亚萨的子孙一百四十八名。
chapters.en: The singers: the children of Asaph, an hundred forty and eight.

chapters.zh: 守门的：沙龙的子孙、亚特的子孙、达们的子孙、亚谷的子孙、哈底大的子孙、朔拜的子孙，共一百三十八名。
chapters.en: The porters: the children of Shallum, the children of Ater, the children of Talmon, the children of Akkub, the children of Hatita, the children of Shobai, an hundred thirty and eight.

chapters.zh: 尼提宁（就是殿役）：西哈的子孙、哈苏巴的子孙、答巴俄的子孙、
chapters.en: The Nethinims: the children of Ziha, the children of Hashupha, the children of Tabbaoth,

chapters.zh: 基绿的子孙、西亚的子孙、巴顿的子孙、
chapters.en: The children of Keros, the children of Sia, the children of Padon,

chapters.zh: 利巴拿的子孙、哈迦巴的子孙、萨买的子孙、
chapters.en: The children of Lebana, the children of Hagaba, the children of Shalmai,

chapters.zh: 哈难的子孙、吉德的子孙、迦哈的子孙、
chapters.en: The children of Hanan, the children of Giddel, the children of Gahar,

chapters.zh: 利亚雅的子孙、利汛的子孙、尼哥大的子孙、
chapters.en: The children of Reaiah, the children of Rezin, the children of Nekoda,

chapters.zh: 迦散的子孙、乌撒的子孙、巴西亚的子孙、
chapters.en: The children of Gazzam, the children of Uzza, the children of Phaseah,

chapters.zh: 比赛的子孙、米乌宁的子孙、尼普心的子孙、
chapters.en: The children of Besai, the children of Meunim, the children of Nephishesim,

chapters.zh: 巴卜的子孙、哈古巴的子孙、哈忽的子孙、
chapters.en: The children of Bakbuk, the children of Hakupha, the children of Harhur,

chapters.zh: 巴洗律的子孙、米希大的子孙、哈沙的子孙、
chapters.en: The children of Bazlith, the children of Mehida, the children of Harsha,

chapters.zh: 巴柯的子孙、西西拉的子孙、答玛的子孙、
chapters.en: The children of Barkos, the children of Sisera, the children of Tamah,

chapters.zh: 尼细亚的子孙、哈提法的子孙。
chapters.en: The children of Neziah, the children of Hatipha.

chapters.zh: 所罗门仆人的后裔，就是琐太的子孙、琐斐列的子孙、比路大的子孙、
chapters.en: The children of Solomon's servants: the children of Sotai, the children of Sophereth, the children of Perida,

chapters.zh: 雅拉的子孙、达昆的子孙、吉德的子孙、
chapters.en: The children of Jaala, the children of Darkon, the children of Giddel,

chapters.zh: 示法提雅的子孙、哈替的子孙、玻黑列哈斯巴音的子孙、亚们的子孙。
chapters.en: The children of Shephatiah, the children of Hattil, the children of Pochereth of Zebaim, the children of Amon.

chapters.zh: 尼提宁和所罗门仆人的后裔共三百九十二名。
chapters.en: All the Nethinims, and the children of Solomon's servants, three hundred ninety and two.

chapters.zh: 从特米拉、特哈萨、基绿、亚顿、音麦上来的，不能指明他们的宗族谱系是以色列人不是；
chapters.en: And these they which went up from Telmelah, Telharesha, Cherub, Addon, and Immer: but they could not shew their father's house, nor their seed, whether they of Israel.

chapters.zh: 他们是第莱雅的子孙、多比雅的子孙、尼哥大的子孙，共六百四十二名。
chapters.en: The children of Delaiah, the children of Tobiah, the children of Nekoda, six hundred forty and two.

chapters.zh: 祭司中，哈巴雅的子孙、哈哥斯的子孙、巴西莱的子孙；因为他们的先祖娶了基列人巴西莱的女儿为妻，所以起名叫巴西莱。
chapters.en: And of the priests: the children of Habaiah, the children of Koz, the children of Barzillai, which took of the daughters of Barzillai the Gileadite to wife, and was called after their name.

chapters.zh: 这三家的人在族谱之中寻查自己的谱系，却寻不著，因此算为不洁，不准供祭司的职任。
chapters.en: These sought their register those that were reckoned by genealogy, but it was not found: therefore were they, as polluted, put from the priesthood.

chapters.zh: 省长对他们说：不可吃至圣的物，直到有用乌陵和土明决疑的祭司兴起来。
chapters.en: And the Tirshatha said unto them, that they should not eat of the most holy things, till there stood a priest with Urim and Thummim.

chapters.zh: 会众共有四万二千三百六十名。
chapters.en: The whole congregation together forty and two thousand three hundred and threescore,

chapters.zh: 此外，还有他们的仆婢七千三百三十七名，又有歌唱的男女二百四十五名。
chapters.en: Beside their manservants and their maidservants, of whom seven thousand three hundred thirty and seven: and they had two hundred forty and five singing men and singing women.

chapters.zh: 他们有马七百三十六匹，骡子二百四十五匹，
chapters.en: Their horses, seven hundred thirty and six: their mules, two hundred forty and five:

chapters.zh: 骆驼四百三十五只，驴六千七百二十匹。
chapters.en: camels, four hundred thirty and five: six thousand seven hundred and twenty asses.

chapters.zh: 有些族长为工程捐助。省长捐入库中的金子一千达利克，碗五十个，祭司的礼服五百三十件。
chapters.en: And some of the chief of the fathers gave unto the work. The Tirshatha gave to the treasure a thousand drams of gold, fifty basons, five hundred and thirty priests' garments.

chapters.zh: 又有族长捐入工程库的金子二万达利克，银子二千二百弥拿。
chapters.en: And of the chief of the fathers gave to the treasure of the work twenty thousand drams of gold, and two thousand and two hundred pound of silver.

chapters.zh: 其余百姓所捐的金子二万达利克，银子二千弥拿，祭司的礼服六十七件。
chapters.en: And which the rest of the people gave twenty thousand drams of gold, and two thousand pound of silver, and threescore and seven priests' garments.

chapters.zh: 于是祭司、利未人、守门的、歌唱的、民中的一些人、尼提宁，并以色列众人，各住在自己的城里。
chapters.en: So the priests, and the Levites, and the porters, and the singers, and of the people, and the Nethinims, and all Israel, dwelt in their cities; and when the seventh month came, the children of Israel in their cities.

chapters.zh: 到了七月，以色列人住在自己的城里。那时，他们如同一人聚集在水门前的宽阔处，请文士以斯拉将耶和华藉摩西传给以色列人的律法书带来。
chapters.en: And all the people gathered themselves together as one man into the street that before the water gate; and they spake unto Ezra the scribe to bring the book of the law of Moses, which the LORD had commanded to Israel.

chapters.zh: 七月初一日，祭司以斯拉将律法书带到听了能明白的男女会众面前。
chapters.en: And Ezra the priest brought the law before the congregation both of men and women, and all that could hear with understanding, upon the first day of the seventh month.

chapters.zh: 在水门前的宽阔处，从清早到晌午，在众男女、一切听了能明白的人面前读这律法书。众民侧耳而听。
chapters.en: And he read therein before the street that before the water gate from the morning until midday, before the men and the women, and those that could understand; and the ears of all the people unto the book of the law.

chapters.zh: 文士以斯拉站在为这事特备的木台上。玛他提雅、示玛、亚奈雅、乌利亚、希勒家，和玛西雅站在他的右边；毗大雅、米沙利、玛基雅、哈顺、哈拔大拿、撒迦利亚，和米书兰站在他的左边。
chapters.en: And Ezra the scribe stood upon a pulpit of wood, which they had made for the purpose; and beside him stood Mattithiah, and Shema, and Anaiah, and Urijah, and Hilkiah, and Maaseiah, on his right hand; and on his left hand, Pedaiah, and Mishael, and Malchiah, and Hashum, and Hashbadana, Zechariah, Meshullam.

chapters.zh: 以斯拉站在众民以上，在众民眼前展开这书。他一展开，众民就都站起来。
chapters.en: And Ezra opened the book in the sight of all the people; (for he was above all the people;) and when he opened it, all the people stood up:

chapters.zh: 以斯拉称颂耶和华至大的神；众民都举手应声说：阿们！阿们！就低头，面伏于地，敬拜耶和华。
chapters.en: And Ezra blessed the LORD, the great God. And all the people answered, Amen, Amen, with lifting up their hands: and they bowed their heads, and worshipped the LORD with faces to the ground.

chapters.zh: 耶书亚、巴尼、示利比、雅悯、亚谷、沙比太、荷第雅、玛西雅、基利他、亚撒利雅、约撒拔、哈难、毗莱雅，和利未人使百姓明白律法；百姓都站在自己的地方。
chapters.en: Also Jeshua, and Bani, and Sherebiah, Jamin, Akkub, Shabbethai, Hodijah, Maaseiah, Kelita, Azariah, Jozabad, Hanan, Pelaiah, and the Levites, caused the people to understand the law: and the people in their place.

chapters.zh: 他们清清楚楚地念神的律法书，讲明意思，使百姓明白所念的。
chapters.en: So they read in the book in the law of God distinctly, and gave the sense, and caused to understand the reading.

chapters.zh: 省长尼希米和作祭司的文士以斯拉，并教训百姓的利未人，对众民说：今日是耶和华 ─ 你们神的圣日，不要悲哀哭泣。这是因为众民听见律法书上的话都哭了；
chapters.en: And Nehemiah, which the Tirshatha, and Ezra the priest the scribe, and the Levites that taught the people, said unto all the people, This day holy unto the LORD your God; mourn not, nor weep. For all the people wept, when they heard the words of the law.

chapters.zh: 又对他们说：你们去吃肥美的，喝甘甜的，有不能预备的就分给他，因为今日是我们主的圣日。你们不要忧愁，因靠耶和华而得的喜乐是你们的力量。
chapters.en: Then he said unto them, Go your way, eat the fat, and drink the sweet, and send portions unto them for whom nothing is prepared: for day holy unto our Lord: neither be ye sorry; for the joy of the LORD is your strength.

chapters.zh: 于是利未人使众民静默，说：今日是圣日；不要作声，也不要忧愁。
chapters.en: So the Levites stilled all the people, saying, Hold your peace, for the day holy; neither be ye grieved.

chapters.zh: 众民都去吃喝，也分给人，大大快乐，因为他们明白所教训他们的话。
chapters.en: And all the people went their way to eat, and to drink, and to send portions, and to make great mirth, because they had understood the words that were declared unto them.

chapters.zh: 次日，众民的族长、祭司，和利未人都聚集到文士以斯拉那里，要留心听律法上的话。
chapters.en: And on the second day were gathered together the chief of the fathers of all the people, the priests, and the Levites, unto Ezra the scribe, even to understand the words of the law.

chapters.zh: 他们见律法上写著，耶和华藉摩西吩咐以色列人要在七月节住棚，
chapters.en: And they found written in the law which the LORD had commanded by Moses, that the children of Israel should dwell in booths in the feast of the seventh month:

chapters.zh: 并要在各城和耶路撒冷宣传报告说：你们当上山，将橄榄树、野橄榄树、番石榴树、棕树，和各样茂密树的枝子取来，照著所写的搭棚。
chapters.en: And that they should publish and proclaim in all their cities, and in Jerusalem, saying, Go forth unto the mount, and fetch olive branches, and pine branches, and myrtle branches, and palm branches, and branches of thick trees, to make booths, as written.

chapters.zh: 于是百姓出去，取了树枝来，各人在自己的房顶上，或院内，或神殿的院内，或水门的宽阔处，或以法莲门的宽阔处搭棚。
chapters.en: So the people went forth, and brought , and made themselves booths, every one upon the roof of his house, and in their courts, and in the courts of the house of God, and in the street of the water gate, and in the street of the gate of Ephraim.

chapters.zh: 从掳到之地归回的全会众就搭棚，住在棚里。从嫩的儿子约书亚的时候直到这日，以色列人没有这样行。于是众人大大喜乐。
chapters.en: And all the congregation of them that were come again out of the captivity made booths, and sat under the booths: for since the days of Jeshua the son of Nun unto that day had not the children of Israel done so. And there was very great gladness.

chapters.zh: 从头一天直到末一天，以斯拉每日念神的律法书。众人守节七日，第八日照例有严肃会。
chapters.en: Also day by day, from the first day unto the last day, he read in the book of the law of God. And they kept the feast seven days; and on the eighth day a solemn assembly, according unto the manner.

chapters.zh: 这月二十四日，以色列人聚集禁食，身穿麻衣，头蒙灰尘。
chapters.en: Now in the twenty and fourth day of this month the children of Israel were assembled with fasting, and with sackclothes, and earth upon them.

chapters.zh: 以色列人（人：原文是种类）就与一切外邦人离绝，站著承认自己的罪恶和列祖的罪孽。
chapters.en: And the seed of Israel separated themselves from all strangers, and stood and confessed their sins, and the iniquities of their fathers.

chapters.zh: 那日的四分之一站在自己的地方念耶和华 ─ 他们神的律法书，又四分之一认罪，敬拜耶和华 ─ 他们的神。
chapters.en: And they stood up in their place, and read in the book of the law of the LORD their God fourth part of the day; and fourth part they confessed, and worshipped the LORD their God.

chapters.zh: 耶书亚、巴尼、甲篾、示巴尼、布尼、示利比、巴尼、基拿尼站在利未人的台上，大声哀求耶和华 ─ 他们的神。
chapters.en: Then stood up upon the stairs, of the Levites, Jeshua, and Bani, Kadmiel, Shebaniah, Bunni, Sherebiah, Bani, Chenani, and cried with a loud voice unto the LORD their God.

chapters.zh: 利未人耶书亚、甲篾、巴尼、哈沙尼、示利比、荷第雅、示巴尼、毗他希雅说：你们要站起来称颂耶和华 ─ 你们的神，永世无尽。耶和华啊，你荣耀之名是应当称颂的！超乎一切称颂和赞美。
chapters.en: Then the Levites, Jeshua, and Kadmiel, Bani, Hashabniah, Sherebiah, Hodijah, Shebaniah, Pethahiah, said, Stand up bless the LORD your God for ever and ever: and blessed be thy glorious name, which is exalted above all blessing and praise.

chapters.zh: 你，惟独你是耶和华！你造了天和天上的天，并天上的万象，地和地上的万物，海和海中所有的；这一切都是你所保存的。天军也都敬拜你。
chapters.en: Thou, thou, LORD alone; thou hast made heaven, the heaven of heavens, with all their host, the earth, and all that therein, the seas, and all that therein, and thou preservest them all; and the host of heaven worshippeth thee.

chapters.zh: 你是耶和华神，曾拣选亚伯兰，领他出迦勒底的吾珥，给他改名叫亚伯拉罕。
chapters.en: Thou the LORD the God, who didst choose Abram, and broughtest him forth out of Ur of the Chaldees, and gavest him the name of Abraham;

chapters.zh: 你见他在你面前心里诚实，就与他立约，应许把迦南人、赫人、亚摩利人、比利洗人、耶布斯人、革迦撒人之地赐给他的后裔，且应验了你的话，因为你是公义的。
chapters.en: And foundest his heart faithful before thee, and madest a covenant with him to give the land of the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Amorites, and the Perizzites, and the Jebusites, and the Girgashites, to give , to his seed, and hast performed thy words; for thou righteous:

chapters.zh: 你曾看见我们列祖在埃及所受的困苦，垂听他们在红海边的哀求，
chapters.en: And didst see the affliction of our fathers in Egypt, and heardest their cry by the Red sea;

chapters.zh: 就施行神迹奇事在法老和他一切臣仆，并他国中的众民身上。你也得了名声，正如今日一样，因为你知道他们向我们列祖行事狂傲。
chapters.en: And shewedst signs and wonders upon Pharaoh, and on all his servants, and on all the people of his land: for thou knewest that they dealt proudly against them. So didst thou get thee a name, as this day.

chapters.zh: 你又在我们列祖面前把海分开，使他们在海中行走干地，将追赶他们的人抛在深海，如石头抛在大水中；
chapters.en: And thou didst divide the sea before them, so that they went through the midst of the sea on the dry land; and their persecutors thou threwest into the deeps, as a stone into the mighty waters.

chapters.zh: 并且白昼用云柱引导他们，黑夜用火柱照亮他们当行的路。
chapters.en: Moreover thou leddest them in the day by a cloudy pillar; and in the night by a pillar of fire, to give them light in the way wherein they should go.

chapters.zh: 你也降临在西乃山，从天上与他们说话，赐给他们正直的典章、真实的律法、美好的条例与诫命，
chapters.en: Thou camest down also upon mount Sinai, and spakest with them from heaven, and gavest them right judgments, and true laws, good statutes and commandments:

chapters.zh: 又使他们知道你的安息圣日，并藉你仆人摩西传给他们诫命、条例、律法。
chapters.en: And madest known unto them thy holy sabbath, and commandedst them precepts, statutes, and laws, by the hand of Moses thy servant:

chapters.zh: 从天上赐下粮食充他们的饥，从磐石使水流出解他们的渴，又吩咐他们进去得你起誓应许赐给他们的地。
chapters.en: And gavest them bread from heaven for their hunger, and broughtest forth water for them out of the rock for their thirst, and promisedst them that they should go in to possess the land which thou hadst sworn to give them.

chapters.zh: 但我们的列祖行事狂傲，硬著颈项不听从你的诫命；
chapters.en: But they and our fathers dealt proudly, and hardened their necks, and hearkened not to thy commandments,

chapters.zh: 不肯顺从，也不记念你在他们中间所行的奇事，竟硬著颈项，居心背逆，自立首领，要回他们为奴之地。但你是乐意饶恕人，有恩典，有怜悯，不轻易发怒，有丰盛慈爱的神，并不丢弃他们。
chapters.en: And refused to obey, neither were mindful of thy wonders that thou didst among them; but hardened their necks, and in their rebellion appointed a captain to return to their bondage: but thou a God ready to pardon, gracious and merciful, slow to anger, and of great kindness, and forsookest them not.

chapters.zh: 他们虽然铸了一只牛犊，彼此说这是领你出埃及的神，因而大大惹动你的怒气；
chapters.en: Yea, when they had made them a molten calf, and said, This thy God that brought thee up out of Egypt, and had wrought great provocations;

chapters.zh: 你还是大施怜悯，在旷野不丢弃他们。白昼，云柱不离开他们，仍引导他们行路；黑夜，火柱也不离开他们，仍照亮他们当行的路。
chapters.en: Yet thou in thy manifold mercies forsookest them not in the wilderness: the pillar of the cloud departed not from them by day, to lead them in the way; neither the pillar of fire by night, to shew them light, and the way wherein they should go.

chapters.zh: 你也赐下你良善的灵教训他们；未尝不赐吗哪使他们糊口，并赐水解他们的渴。
chapters.en: Thou gavest also thy good spirit to instruct them, and withheldest not thy manna from their mouth, and gavest them water for their thirst.

chapters.zh: 在旷野四十年，你养育他们，他们就一无所缺：衣服没有穿破，脚也没有肿。
chapters.en: Yea, forty years didst thou sustain them in the wilderness, they lacked nothing; their clothes waxed not old, and their feet swelled not.

chapters.zh: 并且你将列国之地照分赐给他们，他们就得了西宏之地、希实本王之地，和巴珊王噩之地。
chapters.en: Moreover thou gavest them kingdoms and nations, and didst divide them into corners: so they possessed the land of Sihon, and the land of the king of Heshbon, and the land of Og king of Bashan.

chapters.zh: 你也使他们的子孙多如天上的星，带他们到你所应许他们列祖进入得为业之地。
chapters.en: Their children also multipliedst thou as the stars of heaven, and broughtest them into the land, concerning which thou hadst promised to their fathers, that they should go in to possess .

chapters.zh: 这样，他们进去得了那地，你在他们面前制伏那地的居民，就是迦南人；将迦南人和其君王，并那地的居民，都交在他们手里，让他们任意而待。
chapters.en: So the children went in and possessed the land, and thou subduedst before them the inhabitants of the land, the Canaanites, and gavest them into their hands, with their kings, and the people of the land, that they might do with them as they would.

chapters.zh: 他们得了坚固的城邑、肥美的地土、充满各样美物的房屋、凿成的水井、葡萄园、橄榄园，并许多果木树。他们就吃而得饱，身体肥胖，因你的大恩，心中快乐。
chapters.en: And they took strong cities, and a fat land, and possessed houses full of all goods, wells digged, vineyards, and oliveyards, and fruit trees in abundance: so they did eat, and were filled, and became fat, and delighted themselves in thy great goodness.

chapters.zh: 然而，他们不顺从，竟背叛你，将你的律法丢在背后，杀害那劝他们归向你的众先知，大大惹动你的怒气。
chapters.en: Nevertheless they were disobedient, and rebelled against thee, and cast thy law behind their backs, and slew thy prophets which testified against them to turn them to thee, and they wrought great provocations.

chapters.zh: 所以你将他们交在敌人的手中，磨难他们。他们遭难的时候哀求你，你就从天上垂听，照你的大怜悯赐给他们拯救者，救他们脱离敌人的手。
chapters.en: Therefore thou deliveredst them into the hand of their enemies, who vexed them: and in the time of their trouble, when they cried unto thee, thou heardest from heaven; and according to thy manifold mercies thou gavest them saviours, who saved them out of the hand of their enemies.

chapters.zh: 但他们得平安之后，又在你面前行恶，所以你丢弃他们在仇敌的手中，使仇敌辖制他们。然而他们转回哀求你，你仍从天上垂听，屡次照你的怜悯拯救他们，
chapters.en: But after they had rest, they did evil again before thee: therefore leftest thou them in the hand of their enemies, so that they had the dominion over them: yet when they returned, and cried unto thee, thou heardest from heaven; and many times didst thou deliver them according to thy mercies;

chapters.zh: 又警戒他们，要使他们归服你的律法。他们却行事狂傲，不听从你的诫命，干犯你的典章（人若遵行就必因此活著），扭转肩头，硬著颈项，不肯听从。
chapters.en: And testifiedst against them, that thou mightest bring them again unto thy law: yet they dealt proudly, and hearkened not unto thy commandments, but sinned against thy judgments, (which if a man do, he shall live in them;) and withdrew the shoulder, and hardened their neck, and would not hear.

chapters.zh: 但你多年宽容他们，又用你的灵藉众先知劝戒他们，他们仍不听从，所以你将他们交在列国之民的手中。
chapters.en: Yet many years didst thou forbear them, and testifiedst against them by thy spirit in thy prophets: yet would they not give ear: therefore gavest thou them into the hand of the people of the lands.

chapters.zh: 然而你大发怜悯，不全然灭绝他们，也不丢弃他们；因为你是有恩典、有怜悯的神。
chapters.en: Nevertheless for thy great mercies' sake thou didst not utterly consume them, nor forsake them; for thou a gracious and merciful God.

chapters.zh: 我们的神啊，你是至大、至能、至可畏、守约施慈爱的神。我们的君王、首领、祭司、先知、列祖，和你的众民，从亚述列王的时候直到今日所遭遇的苦难，现在求你不要以为小。
chapters.en: Now therefore, our God, the great, the mighty, and the terrible God, who keepest covenant and mercy, let not all the trouble seem little before thee, that hath come upon us, on our kings, on our princes, and on our priests, and on our prophets, and on our fathers, and on all thy people, since the time of the kings of Assyria unto this day.

chapters.zh: 在一切临到我们的事上，你却是公义的；因你所行的是诚实，我们所做的是邪恶。
chapters.en: Howbeit thou just in all that is brought upon us; for thou hast done right, but we have done wickedly:

chapters.zh: 我们的君王、首领、祭司、列祖都不遵守你的律法，不听从你的诫命和你警戒他们的话。
chapters.en: Neither have our kings, our princes, our priests, nor our fathers, kept thy law, nor hearkened unto thy commandments and thy testimonies, wherewith thou didst testify against them.

chapters.zh: 他们在本国里沾你大恩的时候，在你所赐给他们这广大肥美之地上不事奉你，也不转离他们的恶行。
chapters.en: For they have not served thee in their kingdom, and in thy great goodness that thou gavest them, and in the large and fat land which thou gavest before them, neither turned they from their wicked works.

chapters.zh: 我们现今作了奴仆；至于你所赐给我们列祖享受其上的土产，并美物之地，看哪，我们在这地上作了奴仆！
chapters.en: Behold, we servants this day, and the land that thou gavest unto our fathers to eat the fruit thereof and the good thereof, behold, we servants in it:

chapters.zh: 这地许多出产归了列王，就是你因我们的罪所派辖制我们的。他们任意辖制我们的身体和牲畜，我们遭了大难。
chapters.en: And it yieldeth much increase unto the kings whom thou hast set over us because of our sins: also they have dominion over our bodies, and over our cattle, at their pleasure, and we in great distress.

chapters.zh: 因这一切的事，我们立确实的约，写在册上。我们的首领、利未人，和祭司都签了名。
chapters.en: And because of all this we make a sure , and write ; and our princes, Levites, priests, seal .

chapters.zh: 签名的是：哈迦利亚的儿子 ─ 省长尼希米，和西底家；
chapters.en: Now those that sealed , Nehemiah, the Tirshatha, the son of Hachaliah, and Zidkijah,

chapters.zh: 祭司：西莱雅、亚撒利雅、耶利米、
chapters.en: Seraiah, Azariah, Jeremiah,

chapters.zh: 巴施户珥、亚玛利雅、玛基雅、
chapters.en: Pashur, Amariah, Malchijah,

chapters.zh: 哈突、示巴尼、玛鹿、
chapters.en: Hattush, Shebaniah, Malluch,

chapters.zh: 哈琳、米利末、俄巴底亚、
chapters.en: Harim, Meremoth, Obadiah,

chapters.zh: 但以理、近顿、巴录、
chapters.en: Daniel, Ginnethon, Baruch,

chapters.zh: 米书兰、亚比雅、米雅民、
chapters.en: Meshullam, Abijah, Mijamin,

chapters.zh: 玛西亚、璧该、示玛雅；
chapters.en: Maaziah, Bilgai, Shemaiah: these the priests.

chapters.zh: 又有利未人，就是亚散尼的儿子耶书亚、希拿达的子孙宾内、甲篾；
chapters.en: And the Levites: both Jeshua the son of Azaniah, Binnui of the sons of Henadad, Kadmiel;

chapters.zh: 还有他们的弟兄示巴尼、荷第雅、基利他、毗莱雅、哈难、
chapters.en: And their brethren, Shebaniah, Hodijah, Kelita, Pelaiah, Hanan,

chapters.zh: 米迦、利合、哈沙比雅、
chapters.en: Micha, Rehob, Hashabiah,

chapters.zh: 撒刻、示利比、示巴尼、
chapters.en: Zaccur, Sherebiah, Shebaniah,

chapters.zh: 荷第雅、巴尼、比尼努；
chapters.en: Hodijah, Bani, Beninu.

chapters.zh: 又有民的首领，就是巴录、巴哈摩押、以拦、萨土、巴尼、
chapters.en: The chief of the people; Parosh, Pahathmoab, Elam, Zatthu, Bani,

chapters.zh: 布尼、押甲、比拜、
chapters.en: Bunni, Azgad, Bebai,

chapters.zh: 亚多尼雅、比革瓦伊、亚丁、
chapters.en: Adonijah, Bigvai, Adin,

chapters.zh: 亚特、希西家、押朔、
chapters.en: Ater, Hizkijah, Azzur,

chapters.zh: 荷第雅、哈顺、比赛、
chapters.en: Hodijah, Hashum, Bezai,

chapters.zh: 哈拉、亚拿突、尼拜、
chapters.en: Hariph, Anathoth, Nebai,

chapters.zh: 抹比押、米书兰、希悉、
chapters.en: Magpiash, Meshullam, Hezir,

chapters.zh: 米示萨别、撒督、押杜亚、
chapters.en: Meshezabeel, Zadok, Jaddua,

chapters.zh: 毗拉提、哈难、亚奈雅、
chapters.en: Pelatiah, Hanan, Anaiah,

chapters.zh: 何细亚、哈拿尼雅、哈述、
chapters.en: Hoshea, Hananiah, Hashub,

chapters.zh: 哈罗黑、毗利哈、朔百、
chapters.en: Hallohesh, Pileha, Shobek,

chapters.zh: 利宏、哈沙拿、玛西雅、
chapters.en: Rehum, Hashabnah, Maaseiah,

chapters.zh: 亚希雅、哈难、亚难、
chapters.en: And Ahijah, Hanan, Anan,

chapters.zh: 玛鹿、哈琳、巴拿。
chapters.en: Malluch, Harim, Baanah.

chapters.zh: 其余的民、祭司、利未人、守门的、歌唱的、尼提宁，和一切离绝邻邦居民归服神律法的，并他们的妻子、儿女，凡有知识能明白的，
chapters.en: And the rest of the people, the priests, the Levites, the porters, the singers, the Nethinims, and all they that had separated themselves from the people of the lands unto the law of God, their wives, their sons, and their daughters, every one having knowledge, and having understanding;

chapters.zh: 都随从他们贵胄的弟兄，发咒起誓，必遵行神藉他仆人摩西所传的律法，谨守遵行耶和华 ─ 我们主的一切诫命、典章、律例；
chapters.en: They clave to their brethren, their nobles, and entered into a curse, and into an oath, to walk in God's law, which was given by Moses the servant of God, and to observe and do all the commandments of the LORD our Lord, and his judgments and his statutes;

chapters.zh: 并不将我们的女儿嫁给这地的居民，也不为我们的儿子娶他们的女儿。
chapters.en: And that we would not give our daughters unto the people of the land, nor take their daughters for our sons:

chapters.zh: 这地的居民若在安息日，或甚么圣日，带了货物或粮食来卖给我们，我们必不买。每逢第七年必不耕种，凡欠我们债的必不追讨。
chapters.en: And the people of the land bring ware or any victuals on the sabbath day to sell, we would not buy it of them on the sabbath, or on the holy day: and we would leave the seventh year, and the exaction of every debt.

chapters.zh: 我们又为自己定例，每年各人捐银一舍客勒三分之一，为我们神殿的使用，
chapters.en: Also we made ordinances for us, to charge ourselves yearly with the third part of a shekel for the service of the house of our God;

chapters.zh: 就是为陈设饼、常献的素祭，和燔祭，安息日、月朔、节期所献的与圣物，并以色列人的赎罪祭，以及我们神殿里一切的费用。
chapters.en: For the shewbread, and for the continual meat offering, and for the continual burnt offering, of the sabbaths, of the new moons, for the set feasts, and for the holy , and for the sin offerings to make an atonement for Israel, and all the work of the house of our God.

chapters.zh: 我们的祭司、利未人，和百姓都掣签，看每年是哪一族按定期将献祭的柴奉到我们神的殿里，照著律法上所写的，烧在耶和华 ─ 我们神的坛上。
chapters.en: And we cast the lots among the priests, the Levites, and the people, for the wood offering, to bring into the house of our God, after the houses of our fathers, at times appointed year by year, to burn upon the altar of the LORD our God, as written in the law:

chapters.zh: 又定每年将我们地上初熟的土产和各样树上初熟的果子都奉到耶和华的殿里。
chapters.en: And to bring the firstfruits of our ground, and the firstfruits of all fruit of all trees, year by year, unto the house of the LORD:

chapters.zh: 又照律法上所写的，将我们头胎的儿子和首生的牛羊都奉到我们神的殿，交给我们神殿里供职的祭司；
chapters.en: Also the firstborn of our sons, and of our cattle, as written in the law, and the firstlings of our herds and of our flocks, to bring to the house of our God, unto the priests that minister in the house of our God:

chapters.zh: 并将初熟之麦子所磨的面和举祭、各样树上初熟的果子、新酒与油奉给祭司，收在我们神殿的库房里，把我们地上所产的十分之一奉给利未人，因利未人在我们一切城邑的土产中当取十分之一。
chapters.en: And we should bring the firstfruits of our dough, and our offerings, and the fruit of all manner of trees, of wine and of oil, unto the priests, to the chambers of the house of our God; and the tithes of our ground unto the Levites, that the same Levites might have the tithes in all the cities of our tillage.

chapters.zh: 利未人取十分之一的时候，亚伦的子孙中，当有一个祭司与利未人同在。利未人也当从十分之一中取十分之一，奉到我们神殿的屋子里，收在库房中。
chapters.en: And the priest the son of Aaron shall be with the Levites, when the Levites take tithes: and the Levites shall bring up the tithe of the tithes unto the house of our God, to the chambers, into the treasure house.

chapters.zh: 以色列人和利未人要将五谷、新酒，和油为举祭，奉到收存圣所器皿的屋子里，就是供职的祭司、守门的、歌唱的所住的屋子。这样，我们就不离弃我们神的殿。
chapters.en: For the children of Israel and the children of Levi shall bring the offering of the corn, of the new wine, and the oil, unto the chambers, where the vessels of the sanctuary, and the priests that minister, and the porters, and the singers: and we will not forsake the house of our God.

chapters.zh: 百姓的首领住在耶路撒冷。其余的百姓掣签，每十人中使一人来住在圣城耶路撒冷，那九人住在别的城邑。
chapters.en: And the rulers of the people dwelt at Jerusalem: the rest of the people also cast lots, to bring one of ten to dwell in Jerusalem the holy city, and nine parts in cities.

chapters.zh: 凡甘心乐意住在耶路撒冷的，百姓都为他们祝福。
chapters.en: And the people blessed all the men, that willingly offered themselves to dwell at Jerusalem.

chapters.zh: 以色列人、祭司、利未人、尼提宁，和所罗门仆人的后裔都住在犹大城邑，各在自己的地业中。本省的首领住在耶路撒冷的记在下面：
chapters.en: Now these the chief of the province that dwelt in Jerusalem: but in the cities of Judah dwelt every one in his possession in their cities, , Israel, the priests, and the Levites, and the Nethinims, and the children of Solomon's servants.

chapters.zh: 其中有些犹大人和便雅悯人。犹大人中有法勒斯的子孙、乌西雅的儿子亚他雅。乌西雅是撒迦利雅的儿子；撒迦利雅是亚玛利雅的儿子；亚玛利雅是示法提雅的儿子；示法提雅是玛勒列的儿子。
chapters.en: And at Jerusalem dwelt of the children of Judah, and of the children of Benjamin. Of the children of Judah; Athaiah the son of Uzziah, the son of Zechariah, the son of Amariah, the son of Shephatiah, the son of Mahalaleel, of the children of Perez;

chapters.zh: 又有巴录的儿子玛西雅。巴录是谷何西的儿子；谷何西是哈赛雅的儿子；哈赛雅是亚大雅的儿子；亚大雅是约雅立的儿子；约雅立是撒迦利雅的儿子；撒迦利雅是示罗尼的儿子。
chapters.en: And Maaseiah the son of Baruch, the son of Colhozeh, the son of Hazaiah, the son of Adaiah, the son of Joiarib, the son of Zechariah, the son of Shiloni.

chapters.zh: 住在耶路撒冷、法勒斯的子孙共四百六十八名，都是勇士。
chapters.en: All the sons of Perez that dwelt at Jerusalem four hundred threescore and eight valiant men.

chapters.zh: 便雅悯人中有米书兰的儿子撒路。米书兰是约叶的儿子；约叶是毗大雅的儿子；毗大雅是哥赖雅的儿子；哥赖雅是玛西雅的儿子；玛西雅是以铁的儿子；以铁是耶筛亚的儿子。
chapters.en: And these the sons of Benjamin; Sallu the son of Meshullam, the son of Joed, the son of Pedaiah, the son of Kolaiah, the son of Maaseiah, the son of Ithiel, the son of Jesaiah.

chapters.zh: 其次有迦拜、撒来的子孙，共九百二十八名。
chapters.en: And after him Gabbai, Sallai, nine hundred twenty and eight.

chapters.zh: 细基利的儿子约珥是他们的长官。哈西努亚的儿子犹大是耶路撒冷的副官。
chapters.en: And Joel the son of Zichri their overseer: and Judah the son of Senuah second over the city.

chapters.zh: 祭司中有雅斤，又有约雅立的儿子耶大雅；
chapters.en: Of the priests: Jedaiah the son of Joiarib, Jachin.

chapters.zh: 还有管理神殿的西莱雅。西莱雅是希勒家的儿子；希勒家是米书兰的儿子；米书兰是撒督的儿子；撒督是米拉约的儿子；米拉约是亚希突的儿子。
chapters.en: Seraiah the son of Hilkiah, the son of Meshullam, the son of Zadok, the son of Meraioth, the son of Ahitub, the ruler of the house of God.

chapters.zh: 还有他们的弟兄在殿里供职的，共八百二十二名；又有耶罗罕的儿子亚大雅。耶罗罕是毗拉利的儿子；毗拉利是暗洗的儿子；暗洗是撒迦利亚的儿子；撒迦利亚是巴施户珥的儿子；巴施户珥是玛基雅的儿子。
chapters.en: And their brethren that did the work of the house eight hundred twenty and two: and Adaiah the son of Jeroham, the son of Pelaliah, the son of Amzi, the son of Zechariah, the son of Pashur, the son of Malchiah,

chapters.zh: 还有他的弟兄作族长的，二百四十二名；又有亚萨列的儿子亚玛帅。亚萨列是亚哈赛的儿子；亚哈赛是米实利末的儿子；米实利末是音麦的儿子。
chapters.en: And his brethren, chief of the fathers, two hundred forty and two: and Amashai the son of Azareel, the son of Ahasai, the son of Meshillemoth, the son of Immer,

chapters.zh: 还有他们弟兄、大能的勇士共一百二十八名。哈基多琳的儿子撒巴第业是他们的长官。
chapters.en: And their brethren, mighty men of valour, an hundred twenty and eight: and their overseer Zabdiel, the son of the great men.

chapters.zh: 利未人中有哈述的儿子示玛雅。哈述是押利甘的儿子；押利甘是哈沙比雅的儿子；哈沙比雅是布尼的儿子。
chapters.en: Also of the Levites: Shemaiah the son of Hashub, the son of Azrikam, the son of Hashabiah, the son of Bunni;

chapters.zh: 又有利未人的族长沙比太和约撒拔管理神殿的外事。
chapters.en: And Shabbethai and Jozabad, of the chief of the Levites, the oversight of the outward business of the house of God.

chapters.zh: 祈祷的时候，为称谢领首的是米迦的儿子玛他尼。米迦是撒底的儿子；撒底是亚萨的儿子；又有玛他尼弟兄中的八布迦为副。还有沙母亚的儿子押大。沙母亚是加拉的儿子；加拉是耶杜顿的儿子。
chapters.en: And Mattaniah the son of Micha, the son of Zabdi, the son of Asaph, the principal to begin the thanksgiving in prayer: and Bakbukiah the second among his brethren, and Abda the son of Shammua, the son of Galal, the son of Jeduthun.

chapters.zh: 在圣城的利未人共二百八十四名。
chapters.en: All the Levites in the holy city two hundred fourscore and four.

chapters.zh: 守门的是亚谷和达们，并守门的弟兄，共一百七十二名。
chapters.en: Moreover the porters, Akkub, Talmon, and their brethren that kept the gates, an hundred seventy and two.

chapters.zh: 其余的以色列人、祭司、利未人都住在犹大的一切城邑，各在自己的地业中。
chapters.en: And the residue of Israel, of the priests, the Levites, in all the cities of Judah, every one in his inheritance.

chapters.zh: 尼提宁却住在俄斐勒；西哈和基斯帕管理他们。
chapters.en: But the Nethinims dwelt in Ophel: and Ziha and Gispa over the Nethinims.

chapters.zh: 在耶路撒冷、利未人的长官，管理神殿事务的是歌唱者亚萨的子孙、巴尼的儿子乌西。巴尼是哈沙比雅的儿子；哈沙比雅是玛他尼的儿子；玛他尼是米迦的儿子。
chapters.en: The overseer also of the Levites at Jerusalem Uzzi the son of Bani, the son of Hashabiah, the son of Mattaniah, the son of Micha. Of the sons of Asaph, the singers over the business of the house of God.

chapters.zh: 王为歌唱的出命令，每日供给他们必有一定之粮。
chapters.en: For the king's commandment concerning them, that a certain portion should be for the singers, due for every day.

chapters.zh: 犹大儿子谢拉的子孙、米示萨别的儿子毗他希雅辅助王办理犹大民的事。
chapters.en: And Pethahiah the son of Meshezabeel, of the children of Zerah the son of Judah, at the king's hand in all matters concerning the people.

chapters.zh: 至于村庄和属村庄的田地，有犹大人住在基列亚巴和属基列亚巴的乡村；底本和属底本的乡村；叶甲薛和属叶甲薛的村庄；
chapters.en: And for the villages, with their fields, of the children of Judah dwelt at Kirjatharba, and the villages thereof, and at Dibon, and the villages thereof, and at Jekabzeel, and the villages thereof,

chapters.zh: 耶书亚、摩拉大、伯帕列、
chapters.en: And at Jeshua, and at Moladah, and at Bethphelet,

chapters.zh: 哈萨书亚、别是巴，和属别是巴的乡村；
chapters.en: And at Hazarshual, and at Beersheba, and the villages thereof,

chapters.zh: 洗革拉、米哥拿，和属米哥拿的乡村；
chapters.en: And at Ziklag, and at Mekonah, and in the villages thereof,

chapters.zh: 音临门、琐拉、耶末、
chapters.en: And at Enrimmon, and at Zareah, and at Jarmuth,

chapters.zh: 撒挪亚、亚杜兰，和属这两处的村庄；拉吉和属拉吉的田地；亚西加和属亚西加的乡村。他们所住的地方是从别是巴直到欣嫩谷。
chapters.en: Zanoah, Adullam, and their villages, at Lachish, and the fields thereof, at Azekah, and the villages thereof. And they dwelt from Beersheba unto the valley of Hinnom.

chapters.zh: 便雅悯人从迦巴起，住在密抹、亚雅、伯特利和属伯特利的乡村。
chapters.en: The children also of Benjamin from Geba at Michmash, and Aija, and Bethel, and their villages,

chapters.zh: 亚拿突、挪伯、亚难雅、
chapters.en: at Anathoth, Nob, Ananiah,

chapters.zh: 夏琐、拉玛、基他音、
chapters.en: Hazor, Ramah, Gittaim,

chapters.zh: 哈叠、洗编、尼八拉、
chapters.en: Hadid, Zeboim, Neballat,

chapters.zh: 罗德、阿挪、匠人之谷。
chapters.en: Lod, and Ono, the valley of craftsmen.

chapters.zh: 利未人中有几班曾住在犹大地归于便雅悯的。
chapters.en: And of the Levites divisions Judah, in Benjamin.

chapters.zh: 同著撒拉铁的儿子所罗巴伯和耶书亚回来的祭司与利未人记在下面：祭司是西莱雅、耶利米、以斯拉、
chapters.en: Now these the priests and the Levites that went up with Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel, and Jeshua: Seraiah, Jeremiah, Ezra,

chapters.zh: 亚玛利雅、玛鹿、哈突、
chapters.en: Amariah, Malluch, Hattush,

chapters.zh: 示迦尼、利宏、米利末、
chapters.en: Shechaniah, Rehum, Meremoth,

chapters.zh: 易多、近顿、亚比雅、
chapters.en: Iddo, Ginnetho, Abijah,

chapters.zh: 米雅民、玛底雅、璧迦、
chapters.en: Miamin, Maadiah, Bilgah,

chapters.zh: 示玛雅、约雅立、耶大雅、
chapters.en: Shemaiah, and Joiarib, Jedaiah,

chapters.zh: 撒路、亚木、希勒家、耶大雅。这些人在耶书亚的时候作祭司和他们弟兄的首领。
chapters.en: Sallu, Amok, Hilkiah, Jedaiah. These the chief of the priests and of their brethren in the days of Jeshua.

chapters.zh: 利未人是耶书亚、宾内、甲篾、示利比、犹大、玛他尼。这玛他尼和他的弟兄管理称谢的事。
chapters.en: Moreover the Levites: Jeshua, Binnui, Kadmiel, Sherebiah, Judah, Mattaniah, over the thanksgiving, he and his brethren.

chapters.zh: 他们的弟兄八布迦和乌尼照自己的班次与他们相对。
chapters.en: Also Bakbukiah and Unni, their brethren, over against them in the watches.

chapters.zh: 耶书亚生约雅金；约雅金生以利亚实；以利亚实生耶何耶大；
chapters.en: And Jeshua begat Joiakim, Joiakim also begat Eliashib, and Eliashib begat Joiada,

chapters.zh: 耶何耶大生约拿单；约拿单生押杜亚。
chapters.en: And Joiada begat Jonathan, and Jonathan begat Jaddua.

chapters.zh: 在约雅金的时候，祭司作族长的西莱雅族（或译：班；本段同）有米拉雅；耶利米族有哈拿尼雅；
chapters.en: And in the days of Joiakim were priests, the chief of the fathers: of Seraiah, Meraiah; of Jeremiah, Hananiah;

chapters.zh: 以斯拉族有米书兰；亚玛利雅族有约哈难；
chapters.en: Of Ezra, Meshullam; of Amariah, Jehohanan;

chapters.zh: 米利古族有约拿单；示巴尼族有约瑟；
chapters.en: Of Melicu, Jonathan; of Shebaniah, Joseph;

chapters.zh: 哈琳族有押拿；米拉约族有希勒恺；
chapters.en: Of Harim, Adna; of Meraioth, Helkai;

chapters.zh: 易多族有撒迦利亚；近顿族有米书兰；
chapters.en: Of Iddo, Zechariah; of Ginnethon, Meshullam;

chapters.zh: 亚比雅族有细基利；米拿民族某；摩亚底族有毗勒太；
chapters.en: Of Abijah, Zichri; of Miniamin, of Moadiah, Piltai;

chapters.zh: 璧迦族有沙母亚；示玛雅族有约拿单；
chapters.en: Of Bilgah, Shammua; of Shemaiah, Jehonathan;

chapters.zh: 约雅立族有玛特乃；耶大雅族有乌西；
chapters.en: And of Joiarib, Mattenai; of Jedaiah, Uzzi;

chapters.zh: 撒来族有加莱；亚木族有希伯；
chapters.en: Of Sallai, Kallai; of Amok, Eber;

chapters.zh: 希勒家族有哈沙比雅；耶大雅族有拿坦业。
chapters.en: Of Hilkiah, Hashabiah; of Jedaiah, Nethaneel.

chapters.zh: 至于利未人，当以利亚实、耶何耶大、约哈难、押杜亚的时候，他们的族长记在册上。波斯王大利乌在位的时候，作族长的祭司也记在册上。
chapters.en: The Levites in the days of Eliashib, Joiada, and Johanan, and Jaddua, recorded chief of the fathers: also the priests, to the reign of Darius the Persian.

chapters.zh: 利未人作族长的记在历史上，直到以利亚实的儿子约哈难的时候。
chapters.en: The sons of Levi, the chief of the fathers, written in the book of the chronicles, even until the days of Johanan the son of Eliashib.

chapters.zh: 利未人的族长是哈沙比雅、示利比、甲篾的儿子耶书亚，与他们弟兄的班次相对，照著神人大卫的命令一班一班地赞美称谢。
chapters.en: And the chief of the Levites: Hashabiah, Sherebiah, and Jeshua the son of Kadmiel, with their brethren over against them, to praise to give thanks, according to the commandment of David the man of God, ward over against ward.

chapters.zh: 玛他尼、八布迦、俄巴底亚、米书兰、达们、亚谷是守门的，就是在库房那里守门。
chapters.en: Mattaniah, and Bakbukiah, Obadiah, Meshullam, Talmon, Akkub, porters keeping the ward at the thresholds of the gates.

chapters.zh: 这都是在约撒达的孙子、耶书亚的儿子约雅金和省长尼希米，并祭司文士以斯拉的时候，有职任的。
chapters.en: These in the days of Joiakim the son of Jeshua, the son of Jozadak, and in the days of Nehemiah the governor, and of Ezra the priest, the scribe.

chapters.zh: 耶路撒冷城墙告成的时候，众民就把各处的利未人招到耶路撒冷，要称谢、歌唱、敲钹、鼓瑟、弹琴，欢欢喜喜地行告成之礼。
chapters.en: And at the dedication of the wall of Jerusalem they sought the Levites out of all their places, to bring them to Jerusalem, to keep the dedication with gladness, both with thanksgivings, and with singing, cymbals, psalteries, and with harps.

chapters.zh: 歌唱的人从耶路撒冷的周围和尼陀法的村庄与伯吉甲，又从迦巴和押玛弗的田地聚集，因为歌唱的人在耶路撒冷四围为自己立了村庄。
chapters.en: And the sons of the singers gathered themselves together, both out of the plain country round about Jerusalem, and from the villages of Netophathi;

chapters.zh: a
chapters.en: Also from the house of Gilgal, and out of the fields of Geba and Azmaveth: for the singers had builded them villages round about Jerusalem.

chapters.zh: 祭司和利未人就洁净自己，也洁净百姓和城门，并城墙。
chapters.en: And the priests and the Levites purified themselves, and purified the people, and the gates, and the wall.

chapters.zh: 我带犹大的首领上城，使称谢的人分为两大队，排列而行：第一队在城上往右边向粪厂门行走，
chapters.en: Then I brought up the princes of Judah upon the wall, and appointed two great thanks, went on the right hand upon the wall toward the dung gate:

chapters.zh: 在他们后头的有何沙雅与犹大首领的一半，
chapters.en: And after them went Hoshaiah, and half of the princes of Judah,

chapters.zh: 又有亚撒利雅、以斯拉、米书兰、
chapters.en: And Azariah, Ezra, and Meshullam,

chapters.zh: 犹大、便雅悯、示玛雅、耶利米。
chapters.en: Judah, and Benjamin, and Shemaiah, and Jeremiah,

chapters.zh: 还有些吹号之祭司的子孙，约拿单的儿子撒迦利亚。约拿单是示玛雅的儿子；示玛雅是玛他尼的儿子；玛他尼是米该亚的儿子；米该亚是撒刻的儿子；撒刻是亚萨的儿子；
chapters.en: And of the priests' sons with trumpets; , Zechariah the son of Jonathan, the son of Shemaiah, the son of Mattaniah, the son of Michaiah, the son of Zaccur, the son of Asaph:

chapters.zh: 又有撒迦利亚的弟兄示玛雅、亚撒利、米拉莱、基拉莱、玛艾、拿坦业、犹大、哈拿尼，都拿著神人大卫的乐器，文士以斯拉引领他们。
chapters.en: And his brethren, Shemaiah, and Azarael, Milalai, Gilalai, Maai, Nethaneel, and Judah, Hanani, with the musical instruments of David the man of God, and Ezra the scribe before them.

chapters.zh: 他们经过泉门往前，从大卫城的台阶随地势而上，在大卫宫殿以上，直行到朝东的水门。
chapters.en: And at the fountain gate, which was over against them, they went up by the stairs of the city of David, at the going up of the wall, above the house of David, even unto the water gate eastward.

chapters.zh: 第二队称谢的人要与那一队相迎而行。我和民的一半跟随他们，在城墙上过了炉楼，直到宽墙；
chapters.en: And the other thanks went over against , and I after them, and the half of the people upon the wall, from beyond the tower of the furnaces even unto the broad wall;

chapters.zh: 又过了以法莲门、古门、鱼门、哈楠业楼、哈米亚楼，直到羊门，就在护卫门站住。
chapters.en: And from above the gate of Ephraim, and above the old gate, and above the fish gate, and the tower of Hananeel, and the tower of Meah, even unto the sheep gate: and they stood still in the prison gate.

chapters.zh: 于是，这两队称谢的人连我和官长的一半，站在神的殿里。
chapters.en: So stood the two thanks in the house of God, and I, and the half of the rulers with me:

chapters.zh: 还有祭司以利亚金、玛西雅、米拿民、米该雅、以利约乃、撒迦利亚、哈楠尼亚吹号；
chapters.en: And the priests; Eliakim, Maaseiah, Miniamin, Michaiah, Elioenai, Zechariah, Hananiah, with trumpets;

chapters.zh: 又有玛西雅、示玛雅、以利亚撒、乌西、约哈难、玛基雅、以拦，和以谢奏乐。歌唱的就大声歌唱，伊斯拉希雅管理他们。
chapters.en: And Maaseiah, and Shemaiah, and Eleazar, and Uzzi, and Jehohanan, and Malchijah, and Elam, and Ezer. And the singers sang loud, with Jezrahiah overseer.

chapters.zh: 那日，众人献大祭而欢乐；因为神使他们大大欢乐，连妇女带孩童也都欢乐，甚至耶路撒冷中的欢声听到远处。
chapters.en: Also that day they offered great sacrifices, and rejoiced: for God had made them rejoice with great joy: the wives also and the children rejoiced: so that the joy of Jerusalem was heard even afar off.

chapters.zh: 当日，派人管理库房，将举祭、初熟之物和所取的十分之一，就是按各城田地，照律法所定归给祭司和利未人的分，都收在里头。犹大人因祭司和利未人供职，就欢乐了。
chapters.en: And at that time were some appointed over the chambers for the treasures, for the offerings, for the firstfruits, and for the tithes, to gather into them out of the fields of the cities the portions of the law for the priests and Levites: for Judah rejoiced for the priests and for the Levites that waited.

chapters.zh: 祭司利未人遵守神所吩咐的，并守洁净的礼。歌唱的、守门的，照著大卫和他儿子所罗门的命令也如此行。
chapters.en: And both the singers and the porters kept the ward of their God, and the ward of the purification, according to the commandment of David, of Solomon his son.

chapters.zh: 古时，在大卫和亚萨的日子，有歌唱的伶长，并有赞美称谢神的诗歌。
chapters.en: For in the days of David and Asaph of old chief of the singers, and songs of praise and thanksgiving unto God.

chapters.zh: 当所罗巴伯和尼希米的时候，以色列众人将歌唱的、守门的，每日所当得的分供给他们，又给利未人当得的分；利未人又给亚伦的子孙当得的分。
chapters.en: And all Israel in the days of Zerubbabel, and in the days of Nehemiah, gave the portions of the singers and the porters, every day his portion: and they sanctified unto the Levites; and the Levites sanctified unto the children of Aaron.

chapters.zh: 当日，人念摩西的律法书给百姓听，遇见书上写著说，亚扪人和摩押人永不可入神的会；
chapters.en: On that day they read in the book of Moses in the audience of the people; and therein was found written, that the Ammonite and the Moabite should not come into the congregation of God for ever;

chapters.zh: 因为他们没有拿食物和水来迎接以色列人，且雇了巴兰咒诅他们，但我们的神使那咒诅变为祝福。
chapters.en: Because they met not the children of Israel with bread and with water, but hired Balaam against them, that he should curse them: howbeit our God turned the curse into a blessing.

chapters.zh: 以色列民听见这律法，就与一切闲杂人绝交
chapters.en: Now it came to pass, when they had heard the law, that they separated from Israel all the mixed multitude.

chapters.zh: 先是蒙派管理我们神殿中库房的祭司以利亚实与多比雅结亲，
chapters.en: And before this, Eliashib the priest, having the oversight of the chamber of the house of our God, allied unto Tobiah:

chapters.zh: 便为他预备一间大屋子，就是从前收存素祭、乳香、器皿，和照命令供给利未人、歌唱的、守门的五谷、新酒，和油的十分之一，并归祭司举祭的屋子。
chapters.en: And he had prepared for him a great chamber, where aforetime they laid the meat offerings, the frankincense, and the vessels, and the tithes of the corn, the new wine, and the oil, which was commanded to the Levites, and the singers, and the porters; and the offerings of the priests.

chapters.zh: 那时我不在耶路撒冷；因为巴比伦王亚达薛西三十二年，我回到王那里。过了多日，我向王告假。
chapters.en: But in all this was not I at Jerusalem: for in the two and thirtieth year of Artaxerxes king of Babylon came I unto the king, and after certain days obtained I leave of the king:

chapters.zh: 我来到耶路撒冷，就知道以利亚实为多比雅在神殿的院内预备屋子的那件恶事。
chapters.en: And I came to Jerusalem, and understood of the evil that Eliashib did for Tobiah, in preparing him a chamber in the courts of the house of God.

chapters.zh: 我甚恼怒，就把多比雅的一切家具从屋里都抛出去，
chapters.en: And it grieved me sore: therefore I cast forth all the household stuff of Tobiah out of the chamber.

chapters.zh: 吩咐人洁净这屋子，遂将神殿的器皿和素祭、乳香又搬进去。
chapters.en: Then I commanded, and they cleansed the chambers: and thither brought I again the vessels of the house of God, with the meat offering and the frankincense.

chapters.zh: 我见利未人所当得的分无人供给他们，甚至供职的利未人与歌唱的俱各奔回自己的田地去了。
chapters.en: And I perceived that the portions of the Levites had not been given : for the Levites and the singers, that did the work, were fled every one to his field.

chapters.zh: 我就斥责官长说：为何离弃神的殿呢？我便招聚利未人，使他们照旧供职。
chapters.en: Then contended I with the rulers, and said, Why is the house of God forsaken? And I gathered them together, and set them in their place.

chapters.zh: 犹大众人就把五谷、新酒，和油的十分之一送入库房。
chapters.en: Then brought all Judah the tithe of the corn and the new wine and the oil unto the treasuries.

chapters.zh: 我派祭司示利米雅、文士撒督，和利未人毗大雅作库官管理库房；副官是哈难。哈难是撒刻的儿子；撒刻是玛他尼的儿子。这些人都是忠信的，他们的职分是将所供给的分给他们的弟兄。
chapters.en: And I made treasurers over the treasuries, Shelemiah the priest, and Zadok the scribe, and of the Levites, Pedaiah: and next to them Hanan the son of Zaccur, the son of Mattaniah: for they were counted faithful, and their office to distribute unto their brethren.

chapters.zh: 我的神啊，求你因这事记念我，不要涂抹我为神的殿与其中的礼节所行的善。
chapters.en: Remember me, O my God, concerning this, and wipe not out my good deeds that I have done for the house of my God, and for the offices thereof.

chapters.zh: 那些日子，我在犹大见有人在安息日醡酒（原文是踹酒醡），搬运禾捆驮在驴上，又把酒、葡萄、无花果，和各样的担子在安息日担入耶路撒冷，我就在他们卖食物的那日警戒他们。
chapters.en: In those days saw I in Judah treading wine presses on the sabbath, and bringing in sheaves, and lading asses; as also wine, grapes, and figs, and all burdens, which they brought into Jerusalem on the sabbath day: and I testified in the day wherein they sold victuals.

chapters.zh: 又有推罗人住在耶路撒冷；他们把鱼和各样货物运进来，在安息日卖给犹大人。
chapters.en: There dwelt men of Tyre also therein, which brought fish, and all manner of ware, and sold on the sabbath unto the children of Judah, and in Jerusalem.

chapters.zh: 我就斥责犹大的贵胄说：你们怎么行这恶事犯了安息日呢？
chapters.en: Then I contended with the nobles of Judah, and said unto them, What evil thing this that ye do, and profane the sabbath day?

chapters.zh: 从前你们列祖岂不是这样行，以致我们神使一切灾祸临到我们和这城么？现在你们还犯安息日，使忿怒越发临到以色列！
chapters.en: Did not your fathers thus, and did not our God bring all this evil upon us, and upon this city? yet ye bring more wrath upon Israel by profaning the sabbath.

chapters.zh: 在安息日的前一日，耶路撒冷城门有黑影的时候，我就吩咐人将门关锁，不过安息日不准开放。我又派我几个仆人管理城门，免得有人在安息日担甚么担子进城。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, that when the gates of Jerusalem began to be dark before the sabbath, I commanded that the gates should be shut, and charged that they should not be opened till after the sabbath: and of my servants set I at the gates, there should no burden be brought in on the sabbath day.

chapters.zh: 于是商人和贩卖各样货物的，一两次住宿在耶路撒冷城外。
chapters.en: So the merchants and sellers of all kind of ware lodged without Jerusalem once or twice.

chapters.zh: 我就警戒他们说：你们为何在城外住宿呢？若再这样，我必下手拿办你们。从此以后，他们在安息日不再来了。
chapters.en: Then I testified against them, and said unto them, Why lodge ye about the wall? if ye do again, I will lay hands on you. From that time forth came they no on the sabbath.

chapters.zh: 我吩咐利未人洁净自己，来守城门，使安息日为圣。我的神啊，求你因这事记念我，照你的大慈爱怜恤我。
chapters.en: And I commanded the Levites that they should cleanse themselves, and they should come keep the gates, to sanctify the sabbath day. Remember me, O my God, this also, and spare me according to the greatness of thy mercy.

chapters.zh: 那些日子，我也见犹大人娶了亚实突、亚扪、摩押的女子为妻。
chapters.en: In those days also saw I Jews had married wives of Ashdod, of Ammon, of Moab:

chapters.zh: 他们的儿女说话，一半是亚实突的话，不会说犹大的话，所说的是照著各族的方言。
chapters.en: And their children spake half in the speech of Ashdod, and could not speak in the Jews' language, but according to the language of each people.

chapters.zh: 我就斥责他们，咒诅他们，打了他们几个人，拔下他们的头发，叫他们指著神起誓，必不将自己的女儿嫁给外邦人的儿子，也不为自己和儿子娶他们的女儿。
chapters.en: And I contended with them, and cursed them, and smote certain of them, and plucked off their hair, and made them swear by God, , Ye shall not give your daughters unto their sons, nor take their daughters unto your sons, or for yourselves.

chapters.zh: 我又说：以色列王所罗门不是在这样的事上犯罪么？在多国中并没有一王像他，且蒙他神所爱，神立他作以色列全国的王；然而连他也被外邦女子引诱犯罪。
chapters.en: Did not Solomon king of Israel sin by these things? yet among many nations was there no king like him, who was beloved of his God, and God made him king over all Israel: nevertheless even him did outlandish women cause to sin.

chapters.zh: 如此，我岂听你们行这大恶，娶外邦女子干犯我们的神呢？
chapters.en: Shall we then hearken unto you to do all this great evil, to transgress against our God in marrying strange wives?

chapters.zh: 大祭司以利亚实的孙子、耶何耶大的一个儿子是和伦人参巴拉的女婿，我就从我这里把他赶出去。
chapters.en: And of the sons of Joiada, the son of Eliashib the high priest, son in law to Sanballat the Horonite: therefore I chased him from me.

chapters.zh: 我的神啊，求你记念他们的罪；因为他们玷污了祭司的职任，违背你与祭司利未人所立的约。
chapters.en: Remember them, O my God, because they have defiled the priesthood, and the covenant of the priesthood, and of the Levites.

chapters.zh: 这样，我洁净他们，使他们离绝一切外邦人，派定祭司和利未人的班次，使他们各尽其职。
chapters.en: Thus cleansed I them from all strangers, and appointed the wards of the priests and the Levites, every one in his business;

chapters.zh: 我又派百姓按定期献柴和初熟的土产。我的神啊，求你记念我，施恩与我。
chapters.en: And for the wood offering, at times appointed, and for the firstfruits. Remember me, O my God, for good.
